job.title: Young Professionals Program – Junior Finance Analyst
job.description:
ChampionX Global Business Services is seeking a Junior Analyst to join our Finance team. In this position, you will be responsible for activities associated with maintaining ledger accounts and for developing financial statements and reports.

What’s in it For You:

The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture.

What You Will Do:

You will support daily and monthly financial operations across different Finance areas, such as accounting, payments, collections, master data, and treasury.
Your responsibilities may include assisting with reconciliations, journal entries, reporting, documentation of processes, and contributing to continuous improvement initiatives.

Position Details:

Candidate must reside in: Buenos Aires, Argentina

Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor’s Degree or advanced student in Accounting or Finance.

Advanced level of written and spoken English language

Two years of professional experience in leasing accounting

Experience in ERP Systems (ORACLE/SAP) is desiderable but not required.

Advanced Excel management

No Immigration Sponsorship available for this opportunity

Preferred Qualifications:

Strong analytical skills

High level of accuracy and detail oriented.

Demonstrate ability to prioritize and deliver results within time constraints

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

At ChampionX, we believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran. 

 

In addition, we are committed to furthering the principles of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) through Affirmative Action (AA). Our goal is to fully utilize minority, female, disabled and covered veteran individuals at all levels of the workforce.

job.title: Mobile Fleet Mechanic
job.description:
ChampionX has an immediate need for a Mobile Fleet Mechanic located in North Dakota.  This position is primarily responsible for providing maintenance of any type of heavy duty vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants, and/or warehouse locations. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits.   

  

What’s in it For You:  

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits  
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology  
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path  
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection  
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1  

  

What You Will Do:  

  • Servicing all types of delivery and treating trucks.  
  • Inspect all heavy duty vehicles once a month  
  • Perform scheduled maintenance on all delivery vehicles  
  • Perform field repairs as needed  
  • Take part in planning and scheduling repairs in daily ops meeting  
  • Maintain ChampionX policy inspection maintenance records  
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills  
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment  
  • Assist in any emergency that may need mechanical knowledge  
  • Ensure equipment is safe and in proper working order including scheduling of preventative maintenance  
  • Adhere to company policies and follow Standard Operating Procedure per job task  
  • Wear required personal protection equipment (PPE) at all times during job tasks  
  • Ensure a safe work environment, promote a safety culture, and deliver safety training (weekly), etc.  
  • Manage all fleet equipment and maintenance needs  
  • Directing all fleet mechanics and maintenance activities to ensure all repair and maintenance work is performed safely, efficiently, and timely.  
  • Managing monthly OPEX on repair and maintenance  
  • Manage and perform inventory tasks  
  • Ensure all partners in the supply chain are working effectively and efficiently to ensure smooth operations and maintenance.  

   

Position Details:  

  • This position will be based in Williston or Dickinson, ND. Will require possible travel to locations in the field for repairs.    

   

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED  
  • 2 years diesel mechanic experience  
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)  
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role  
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read, and write English

   

Physical Demands  

  • Must be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50 lbs.  
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing  

   

Preferred Qualifications:  

  • 5 years diesel mechanic experience  
  • Chemical pump and plumbing knowledge   
  • Knowledge of vehicle electrical, hydraulic, and chassis systems  
  • Have certifications in brake, CVSA, and A/C systems  
  • Knowledge of use of diagnostic software (ADOC, INSITE)  
  • Experience working on hydraulic pumps, and PTO  
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill  
  • Class A or B Commercial Driver’s License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides with all the relevant endorsements (X – Hazmat & Tanker)  

  

About ChampionX  

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.  

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

At ChampionX, we believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran. 

 

In addition, we are committed to furthering the principles of Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) through Affirmative Action (AA). Our goal is to fully utilize minority, female, disabled and covered veteran individuals at all levels of the workforce.  

 

We will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws, including the City of Los Angeles’ Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance and the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance.

job.title: Fleet Mechanic
job.description:

We have an immediate need for a Mobile Fleet Mechanic located in Odessa, TX.  This position is primarily responsible for providing maintenance of any type of heavy duty vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants, and/or warehouse locations. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits.

job.title: Account Manager - Upstream Chemicals
job.description:
ChampionX is hiring for future Account Managers and District Sales Representatives in Williston, ND. If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory

  • Assist in performing customer service and account management activities under the guidance and supervision of experienced sales professionals

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer complaints, escalating as required

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX

  • Assist in developing strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives

  • Achieve annual sales target by working collaboratively on targeting new business opportunities through application of professional selling and planning techniques

  • Assist with collection of samples and conduct laboratory analysis

Position Details:

  • Work week and shift:

  • Up to 25% overnight travel required to support sales territory

  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or Equivalent

  • 2 years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role  

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial), or Life Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, etc.)

  • 3+ years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience

  • Experience in oil and gas industry

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills

  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

  • Mechanically Inclined

About Us: ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. For more news and information, visit SLB.com Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration


ChampionX has an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in Triadelphia, WV.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil and gas wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1

  • $10,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis

  • Maintain chemical inventory reports

  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.

  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards

  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections

  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting

  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills

  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment

  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of Triadelphia, WV

  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of Triadelphia, WV

  • Relief travel positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday

  • 10-12 hour day time shifts

  • Some regional travel as needed

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED

  • Must currently hold a valid Class A Commercial's Drivers License, issued by the state in which the candidate resides

  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days

  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required

  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle

  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English

  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role

  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab

  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.

  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required

  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges

  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units

  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)

  • Experience in customer relations

  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds

  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $70,000 to $95,000 per year!

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. job.title: Chemical Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for Chemical Service Technicians in Bakersfield, CA.  If you are a strong operation professional with a proven track record of success working in oilfield, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

What Will You Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  

  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.

  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.

  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 10%

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent

  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s Degree or Trade School Certification

  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience

  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

Position Details:

  • Hourly Compensation: $25 - $28/hr., depending on experience

  • Work Schedule: Monday - Friday, some weekends required

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

job.title: Electronics Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We are looking for a Electronics Technician located in Tulsa, OK. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Uses mastery of electrician trades skills and knowledge to diagnose and solve electrical problems in manufacturing equipment, machines, and equipment.

  • Creates and assembles components and installs them as required.

  • Performs preventative maintenance.

  • Document maintenance and repairs as required.

  • Interacts fluently with those in other trades and work areas to complete work assignments; provides technical electrical expertise to mechanics, facilities, and other work teams.

  • Facilitates repair of electrical and electronic equipment, induction heating.

  • Design electrical and electronic equipment.

  • Edit program changes in automated machines using PLC logic.

  • Maintain parts and materials inventory for electronic equipment.

  • Formulates maintenance of computer programming.

  • Calibrating eddy current inspection equipment and building coils for same.

  • Ensure proper heat control on heat-treating furnaces.

  • Check calibration of measuring devices on RMC.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma, GED or equivalent required with Department of Labor Certification OR Associates degree from an accredited program in Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field or equivalent education and/or experience.

  • 2 years of experience in a similar manufacturing role

  • Good oral and written communication skills, including the ability to follow written and oral instructions.

  • Good math and analytical skills.

  • Good organizational and time management skills.

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite.

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • The employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • The employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.


We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. job.title: Local Hazmat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Operational Specialist located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What's in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil wells and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis

  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report safety hazards

  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections

  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting

  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills

  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment

  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Odessa, TX

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school graduate or GED

  • Must currently hold a valid Class A or B Commercial Driver's License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides

  • Must be willing and able to obtain Hazardous Material and Tanker Endorsement within the first 30 days of employment

  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required

  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)

  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English

  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.

  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required

  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges

  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)

  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds

job.title: District Account Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a District Account Manager located in San Antonio, Texas. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory

  • Develop strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives. Leverage external network to pursue business opportunities within assigned territory

  • Generate and execute sales plans in assigned customer base and new customer accounts to meet defined territory profit increase goals

  • Regularly engage in Health Check (audit) of current accounts

  • Demonstrate strong team leadership by actively participating in District/Regional/Corporate initiatives

  • Collaborate in cross-functional projects within ChampionX, promoting the transfer of knowledge as well as the delivery of innovative processes and programs

  • Support/lead trial of new technologies and proactively show initiative by bringing ideas and new technologies to the District

  • Initiate and lead sales campaigns within District

  • Demonstrate versatility by calling on all levels of the customer organization

  • Lead, coach and mentor less experienced sales representatives and managers on diverse topics

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or Equivalent

  • 7 years of Chemical industry experience

  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • No immigration sponsorship offered for this role (US Only)

  • Up to 10% overnight travel required to support sales territory

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial), or Life Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, etc.)

  • 7 or more years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience

  • 7-10 years of experience directly related to well service stimulation business

  • Experience in oil and gas industry

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • Account leadership experience that includes calling on multilevel plant or facilities management to accomplish company goals

  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills

  • Good negotiation and problem resolution skills

  • Working knowledge of Well Construction, Stimulation, Reservoir and Drilling Fundamental

job.title: SHE Specialist I
job.description:
SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an SHE Specialist based in Dammam, Saudi Arabia. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is dedicated to improving lives and passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement

  • Access to Employee Resource Groups (ERG’s). These grassroots, employee-led groups are open to anyone in the organization and allow employees to come together to explore common interests and foster a diverse and inclusive workplace within the organization

  • Access to multiple knowledge sharing platforms to enhance collaboration and engagement.

What You will do:

  • Follow safety policies to provide a safe workplace for all employees and supports the guiding principles of Goal Zero to continually improve the plant’s safety, health and environmental performance, security, design, development, manufacture, storage, marketing, distribution, use and ultimate safe disposal of chemical products. Drive culture of safety; Report safety observations / incidents.

  • Supports and is committed to the Quality Improvement Process.  Plans strategies to ensure error-free work by “doing it right the first time” and conveys this attitude in the daily performance of various duties, in our drive continuous improvement.

  • Observe safety and quality procedures and proper use of equipment and material. Focus on improving SHE practices in the workplace.

  • Ensure compliance of local regulatory and ChampionX Safety, Health and Environmental standards for Saudi.

  • Ensures timely completion of all government agency and corporate safety, health, & environmental reporting (example ERP, Incident and Injury Management etc), as well as communication with agencies and appropriate personnel.

  • Responsible for site Business Continuity Plan (BCP). Supports management in Crisis Management Plan (CMP).

  • Manage compliance of HSE training plans and programs and delivery training as required by company policy, customer requirements and regulatory bodies.

  • Understand the components of the SHEQ Management Systems; maintain accurate data in accordance with applicable record retention requirements.

  • Implement and maintain company SHEQ documents adhering to local environmental, health and safety regulations.

  • Organize, Lead, and conduct external/internal audits for Integrated Management Systems (ISO 9001:2015, ISO:45001, ISO:14001).

  • Ensures compliance with the requirements necessary to maintain the company's ISO 9001, ISO 14001 and ISO 45001 certification.

  • Ensure case management practices are communicated, implemented, and followed for work related injuries and reasonable accommodations are made to mitigate aggravation of non-work-related injuries.

  • Lead the investigation of incidents and injuries and prepare studies and analyses of industrial incident causes and hazards to health for use by company personnel.

  • Manage the inspection of regional facilities to detect existing or potential incident and health hazards, determine corrective or preventative measures where indicated, and follow up to ensure measures have been implemented and completed.

  • Represent the organization in community or industry safety groups and programs.

  • Oversees the Industrial Hygiene program for the CX personnel.

  • Oversees Contractor Qualification, Evaluation and Safety Program.

  • Conduct and/or assist with risk assessments, incident investigations, and corrective actions.

  • Compile and analyze SHEQ statistics and incident reports. Issue monthly reports.

  • Maintain confidentiality of all matters.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Education & Experience:

  • Diploma in occupational health and safety with 3 years’ experience in the oil and gas or manufacturing industry.

  • Experience of internal auditing and maintenance of ISO standards, documentation, and requirements.

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License.

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite, and ability to learn internal business systems).

  • English language skills.

  • Strong communication and organizational skills.

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multitask.

About us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in El Dorado, KS.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $4,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of El Dorado, KS.
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of El Dorado, KS.
  • Relief positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Multiple Schedules available
  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • Weekend rotation availability with days off in the week
  • 10-12 hour day time shifts


Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A or B Commercial Driver's License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $75,000 to $95,000 a year!

job.title: Assistant Plant Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Assistant Process Operator in our Chemical Technologies group located in Odessa, TX.  This position will join the production team in the Odessa plant and allow you to learn and progress through all operational skills required to blend, react, and packaging areas of the plant.  Reports to the Production Team leads with interaction of other core functional groups in the Odessa ChampionX Manufacturing Plant.

 

If you are a dedicated professional with a proven track record of success, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive base rates and benefits. dedicated professional with a proven track record of success we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive base rates and benefits.

 

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, and drives a safety-focused culture
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more!

 

What You Will Do:

  • Complete work functions within production operations on a daily basis.
  • Responsible for quality blending of finished products and the bulk, drum and packaging of products.
  • General forklift duties required.
  • Core responsibility for blending and packaging of products demands the use of mechanical and electrical equipment such as pump, mixing equipment and automation control systems.
  • Completion and compliance of all safety initiatives and certification requirements including all near miss and incident reporting.
  • Accountable for data entry of production tickets, good receipts and shipping documentation.
  • Daily inspection of work area and monthly document reporting.
  • Daily housekeeping to ensure a clean and organized work environment is maintained at all times.
  • Assist with physical inventory counts and processes.
  • Compliance with all applicable ISO requirements.

 

Minimum Requirements:

  • High School Diploma or GED
  • Must be medically qualified to wear a respirator.
  • Physically able to lift 60 pounds, climb stairs or a ladder, and agile enough to squat and bend over (Agility Tests 1, 2a & 2b).
  • Immigration Sponsorship not offered for this role

 

Preferred Qualification:

  • Team player with ability to perform physical work.
  • Basic computer skills with the ability to navigate core programs.
  • Two year process technology degree a plus.
  • Minimum of 1 years' experience as an operator in a batch or specialty chemicals manufacturing plant.
  • Minimum of 1 year of relevant experience
  • Forklift operation certified
  • 24 hours Hazwoper certification

job.title: Chemist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

Job Characteristics: This is an entry level professional position in which the employee demonstrates the ability to apply basic chemistry principles, theories and concepts to assignments where precedents typically exist. Develops solutions to problems of limited scope and complexity using basic scientific knowledge.

Minimum Qualifications
•    Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Chemistry or a related scientific field
•    0–2 years of relevant experience in an industrial or research laboratory setting
•    Ability to work under direct supervision and follow established protocols
•    Strong communication and documentation skills
•    A commitment to safety, quality, and teamwork


Preferred Qualifications
•    Hands-on experience with analytical instrumentation and sample preparation
•    Experience in oilfield chemistry, chemical manufacturing, or industrial analytical labs
•    Demonstrated problem-solving skills and attention to detail

Independence Level/Reports to: Works under general supervision on assigned tasks or portions of projects. Normally reports to project/program leader or more senior technical leader.

About ChampionX

job.title: Assistant Process Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Assistant Process Operator in our Sugar Land, TX plant. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It for You: 

  • Enjoy paid training allowing you to learn from successful professionals
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, and drives a safety-focused culture
  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best
  • Access to Employee Resource Groups (ERG’s), facilitating collaboration and fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.
  • Utilization of multiple knowledge-sharing platforms to enhance collaboration and engagement across teams.
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do: 

  • Follow safety policies to provide a safe workplace for all employees and supports the guiding principles of Goal Zero to continually improve the plant’s safety, health and environmental performance, security, design, development, manufacture, storage, marketing, distribution, use and ultimate safe disposal of chemical products. Drive culture of safety; Report safety observations / incidents 
  • Supports and is committed to the Quality Improvement Process.  Plans strategies to ensure error-free work by “doing it right the first time” and conveys this attitude in the daily performance of various duties 
  • Identify areas for process improvement within the roles function supporting continuous improvement 
  • Be proficient in use of process equipment
  • Perform all aspects of manufacturing a product from a process order or bill of material
  • Operate of PC to perform various production functions such as generating process orders, inventory checks, label making, and some quality checks
  • Prepare drums, pails, totes, and other containers for transport of products
  • Maintains a clean, safe, and environmentally compliant work and storage area
  • Labels and placards packaged material in accordance with company policy and government regulations
  • Operate pumps, valves, and other processing equipment for safe and efficient chemical production
  • Operate all transporting equipment used to receive raw materials and the transport of products
  • Effectively load and unload bulk materials from tankers
  • Make necessary hose line-ups for loading material from tankers into vessels and vice versa
  • Operate scales and maintain drumming platform
  • Support the processes to meet the requirements of the current ISO and RC Standards
  • Prepare drums and toes for loading and shipping which includes labeling of the container
  • Participate in physical inventory, safety meetings, and improvements committees
  • May perform necessary Quality Assurance tests
  • Be able to act as a second line Production Planner as needed
  • Be able to troubleshoot and repair minor problems and alert maintenance to major repairs
  • Demonstrate successful ability to supervise Assistant Process Operator and Operator
  • Effectively manage through “hand on” example
  • Have final responsibility for production moving out of the plant in an efficient manner, maintaining a 95% on time service level

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma/GED
  • Minimum 1 year manufacturing experience
  • Must have the ability to don a respirator
  • Must have the ability to wear fall protection if necessary
  • Willing and able to work weekends
  • Willing and able to work a rotation and nights
  • Excellent written and verbal communications skills
  • Team Player with ability to perform physical work.
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high, climb stairs or ladders, and agile enough to squat and bend over (Agility Tests 1, 2a, 2b).
  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and is subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Process Technology Degree preferred
  • Transportation experience
  • Forklift experience

About Us: 

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Assistant Process Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Assistant Process Operator located in Freeport, Texas. The Assistant Operator (Packager) supports daily production by safely packaging finished chemical products into approved containers such as drums, totes, and pails. This role ensures all packaged materials meet safety, quality, and regulatory standards. The Assistant Operator works with Operations, Logistics, and Quality to maintain a safe, efficient, and compliant manufacturing environment.

Key Responsibilities

Packaging and Production

  • Operate packaging equipment including fill lines, pumps, valves, cappers, torque tools, scales, and labelers.
  • Package materials in accordance with batch instructions and production schedules.
  • Perform in‑process checks including quality samples for testing, weights, seals, fill accuracy, and container integrity.
  • Prepare drums, totes, and other containers for filling, labeling, and shipment.
  • Assist with equipment startups, shutdowns, changeovers, and minor troubleshooting.
  • Complete accurate electronic and paper production and packaging records.
  • Assist with hose line‑ups for transferring materials to and from tanks or vessels.

Safety, Health, and Environmental Compliance

  • Follow all site safety procedures including PPE, Lockout/Tagout, confined space, and hazard communication.
  • Handle and transfer chemicals safely in accordance with SDSs and site standard operating procedures.
  • Maintain compliance with OSHA, EPA, DOT, ISO, and internal environmental programs.
  • Report hazards, near misses, spills, or equipment issues immediately.
  • Participate in required safety training, meetings, and observations.

Material Handling and Logistics

  • Operate forklifts, pallet jacks, and drum/tote handling equipment.
  • Load and unload packaged goods and raw materials following site protocols.
  • Label, placard, and stage finished products according to regulatory requirements.
  • Support inventory counts, material identification, and documentation accuracy.

Housekeeping and Equipment Care

  • Maintain a clean and organized work area following 5S and housekeeping standards.
  • Perform basic cleaning and routine preventive maintenance on packaging equipment.
  • Participate in shift handovers, inspections, and audit preparation activities.

Required Qualifications

  • High school diploma or GED.
  • Ability to work safely around chemicals and operate industrial equipment.
  • Ability to lift 50+ pounds and perform physically demanding tasks.
  • Ability to wear required PPE including respirators and fall protection.
  • Ability to read, write, and follow detailed procedures in English.
  • Willingness to work rotating shifts, nights, weekends, and overtime.
  • Must pass drug testing for safety‑sensitive roles.

Minimum Qualifications

  • 1 year of manufacturing or industrial experience.
  • Forklift certification or experience operating forklifts.
  • Experience with chemical packaging, pumps, valves, drums, and totes.
  • Experience with SAP or similar production/inventory systems.
  • Familiarity with ISO, GMP, or Responsible Care programs.

Competencies

  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to quality.
  • Safety‑first mindset consistent with site expectations.
  • Effective communication and teamwork.
  • Reliability, initiative, and strong work ethic.
  • Ability to follow procedures and regulatory requirements consistently.

Working Conditions

  • Work performed in a chemical manufacturing environment with exposure to noise, heat, humidity, fumes, and moving equipment.
  • Required use of PPE including safety glasses, gloves, hard hats, and respirators as needed.
  • Work may involve climbing stairs, maneuvering heavy containers, and handling hazardous materials.

Physical Requirements

  • Ability to lift and carry up to 50 pounds.
  • Ability to stand, walk, bend, climb, and perform physical tasks for extended periods.
  • Ability to wear respiratory protection and other PPE as required.

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in McCook, Nebraska.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $4,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of McCook, NE
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of McCook, NE
  • Relief positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Multiple Schedules available
  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • Weekend rotation availability with days off in the week
  • 10-12 hour day time shifts

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A or B Commercial Driver's License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $75,000 to $95,000 a year!

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

job.title: Senior Mechanical Technician
job.description:
ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.


We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We are looking for a Senior Mechanical Technician located in Singapore. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

Job Overview:

The Senior Mechanical Technician is responsible for ensuring the reliability, safety, and performance of mechanical equipment in the plant. The role focuses on preventive and corrective maintenance, troubleshooting, and condition monitoring of rotating and static equipment to minimize downtime and support plant operations.

What You will do:

  • Installation, calibration, inspection, and troubleshooting of mechanical equipment, in accordance with Engineering and Company Standards.

  • Carry out preventive and breakdown maintenance efficiently and effectively for process and utilities equipment and building facilities.

  • Assist in coordinating and ensuring the quality of work carried out by external and internal contractors are as per requirement.

  • Maintain and service rotating equipment such as reactors, pumps, agitators, air compressors and ancillaries.

  • Maintain and service static equipment such as heat-exchangers, vessels, tanks and ancillaries.

  • Perform shaft laser alignment and use vibration analysis tool to collect data.

  • Maintain the mechanical spare parts inventory.

  • Develop written job plans for mechanical projects.

  • Develop written procedures for mechanical equipment maintenance and inspection.

  • Maintain the mechanical spare parts inventory.

  • Plan and direct procurement of repair parts and material for mechanical equipment for project and maintenance purposes.

  • Support Plant Operations to minimize production impacts and enhance reliability.

  • Maintain high standards of workmanship and professionalism while working as a member of a team or when working alone.

  • Participate in reliability / TPM programs to improve availability rates for all process mechanical facilities.

  • Maintain proper records of all jobs performed in the SAP and CMMS application and participate in mechanical related projects and plant modification work.

Minimum Qualifications

  • Nitec or Diploma in Mechanical engineering or Equivalent

  • Minimum 10 years of relevant work experience in process or mechanical disciplines is required, with foundational exposure to instrumentation and electrical trades.

Preferred Qualifications

  • ​Prior experience working in chemical or process plant environments is an advantage.

  • Willingness to work overtime when operational requirements demand.

  • Available to participate in an on‑call rotation to support 24/7/365 facility operations.

  • Must be able to respond to call‑outs within 30 to 60 minutes of notification.

  • Preferred to be able to use and navigate business applications including Computerized Maintenance Management Systems (CMMS) in SAP and proficiency in Microsoft Office applications is preferred.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. job.title: District Representative III
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a District Representative located in Midland, TX. If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales we invite you to apply. The District Representative is responsible for overseeing client relationships and commercial activities related to the midstream, gas treating, and gas processing. You will be primarily responsible for revenue and profit growth of ChampionX programs and services in targeted accounts within the Oil and Gas industries.  You will be required to take a consultative sales approach with a primary emphasis on strong account leadership skills and retention of strategic ChampionX accounts. In addition, you must build long-term relationships with a large customer base by understanding their key business drivers, executing system assurance programs, and selling new technologies.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Act as the primary contact for clients involved in refined product sales, transportation, and logistics.
  • Collaborate with supply chain, logistics, and operations teams to ensure timely and accurate product delivery.
  • Monitor market trends, regulatory changes, and customer feedback to identify opportunities for improvement and growth.
  • Develop and maintain strong client relationships to support retention and expansion.
  • Prepare and present performance reports, forecasts, and proposals to clients and internal stakeholders.
  • Resolve service issues and coordinate with internal teams to ensure customer satisfaction.
  • Support business development initiatives and contribute to strategic planning.

Position Details:

  • Work week and shift:
  • Up to 10% overnight travel required to support sales territory
  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Business or Engineering.
  • Minimum of 3 years of experience in water utility treatment experience.
  • Strong understanding of product specifications, blending, and logistics.
  • Excellent communication, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
  • Proficiency in CRM systems, Microsoft Office Suite, and data analysis tools.
  • Ability to travel to client sites and operational facilities as needed.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Familiarity with logistics and inventory management systems.
  • 1-2 years of successful technical sales or field sales support experience
  • 1-2 years of experience directly related to well service stimulation business
  • Experience in oil and gas industry
  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation
  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills
  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

job.title: Electronics Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. We have an immediate need for an Electronics Technician. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement

What You Will Do:

  • Uses mastery of electrician trades skills and knowledge to diagnose and solve electrical problems in manufacturing equipment, machines, and equipment.

  • Creates and assembles components and installs them as required.

  • Performs preventative maintenance.

  • Document maintenance and repairs as required.

  • Interacts fluently with those in other trades and work areas to complete work assignments; provides technical electrical expertise to mechanics, facilities, and other work teams.

  • Facilitates repair of electrical and electronic equipment, induction heating.

  • Design electrical and electronic equipment.

  • Edit program changes in automated machines using PLC logic.

  • Maintain parts and materials inventory for electronic equipment.

  • Formulates maintenance of computer programming.

  • Calibrating eddy current inspection equipment and building coils for same.

  • Ensure proper heat control on heat-treating furnaces.

  • Check calibration of measuring devices on RMC.

  • Contributes to the department continuous improvement culture.

  • Knows and complies with all safety policies and procedures.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

  • Other duties as required.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma, GED or equivalent required with Department of Labor Certification OR Associates degree from an accredited program in Electrical/Electronic Engineering, or a related field or equivalent education and/or experience.

  • 2 years of experience in a similar manufacturing role

  • Good oral and written communication skills, including the ability to follow written and oral instructions.

  • Good math and analytical skills.

  • Good organizational and time management skills.

  • Good computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite.

Physical Demands:  

  • Must be able to lift and carry 50 lbs regularly.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • The employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • The employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

job.title: Permian Sales Lead - Emissions
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Permian Sales Lead located in Midland, Texas. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

The Permian Sales Lead is responsible for driving revenue growth, supporting strategic sales initiatives, and leading day-to-day customer engagement activities. This role partners closely with cross-functional teams such as Marketing, Operations, Product Development and Customer Success to ensure alignment of customer needs, market opportunities, and commercial priorities. The Permian Sales Lead serves as a key point of contact for clients while guiding internal teams to deliver solutions that support both short- and long-term business objectives.

What's In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Revenue Growth & Pipeline Management:

  • Develop, manage, and grow a robust sales pipeline within assigned markets and accounts.

  • Identify new business opportunities and lead prospecting efforts.

  • Support forecasting, reporting, and performance metrics to inform business decisions.

  • Client Relationship Management

  • Serve as a primary contact for customers, ensuring a high level of satisfaction and proactive communication.

  • Conduct regular client meetings to understand business needs and present tailored solutions.

  • Build long-term partnerships by delivering a consultative sales approach.

  • Sales Strategy & Execution:

  • Partner with leadership to execute sales strategies aligned with company objectives.

  • Analyze market trends, competition, and customer insights to guide commercial decisions.

  • Support pricing strategies and proposal development.

  • Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Work closely with Operations, Product, Marketing, and Finance teams to ensure seamless delivery of customer commitments.

  • Provide customer feedback that informs product development and service enhancements.

  • Leadership & Coaching:

  • Mentor junior sales team members and support onboarding of new hires.

  • Promote a culture of accountability, performance, and continuous improvement.

  • Other duties as required.

  • Maintains confidentiality of all matters.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Environmental Engineering, Atmospheric Sciences, Business or related field, minimum of five years’ experience in Oil & Gas segment with strategic connections with Operators in Permian area.

  • 5 years of experience in sales, business development, or account management; industry experience may be required depending on the role.

  • Proven track record of achieving or exceeding sales targets.

  • Strong communication, negotiation, and presentation skills.

  • Ability to build trusted relationships with both internal and external stakeholders.

  • Proficiency with CRM systems, sales analytics tools, and Microsoft Office Suite.

  • Ability to multitask.

  • Strong attention to detail.

  • Ability to work without direct supervision.

  • Collaborative attitude and strong work ethic with the ability to enthusiastically represent the company.

  • 60-80% availability for inter-regional travel.

  • Service oriented attitude.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 5 years in a similar sales position.

  • Experience and Knowledge of ESG, Emissions and/or Atmospheric Sciences

  • Oil & gas industry experience.

  • History of working in a field that requires autonomy and self-motivation.

  • Prior experience demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task.

  • Prior experience that required excellent organizational skills.

job.title: Chemical Service Technician
job.description:
isclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

Job Posting Description

ChampionX has immediate and future needs for a Chemical Service Technician located in Baker, MT. If you are a strong operation professional with a proven track record of success working in oilfield, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly Rate of $28-$32

What Will You Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  
  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.
  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.
  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.
  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.
  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 10%

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s Degree or Trade School Certification
  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience
  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude
  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience
  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.
  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation
  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.
