job.title: CEOR Group Leader
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate opening for an RD&E Group Leader for our Chemically Enhanced Oil Recovery (CEOR) Technology team in Sugar Land, TX. This individual will be responsible for leading the development and deployment of innovative technology solutions to achieve Recovery related business objectives. The CEOR RD&E Group Leader will report to the CEOR Technology Manager for the PCT Technology team and work in close collaboration with global business, Technology, New Product Development (NPD), Marketing, and Sales counterparts ensure inter-team and global connectivity.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Lead the CEOR Technology team by ensuring all team members perform their duties safely, efficiently, and in alignment with quality standards, while fostering innovation and continuous technical and personal/professional growth.

  • Lead and coordinate Technology resources both on direct team in Sugar Land, TX and globally in other locations in accordance with business strategy, needs, and impact.

  • Develops organizational talent and succession plans and provides ongoing coaching and mentoring for staff. Owns the strategy for team and organizational development, including safety and employee engagement.

  • Ensures ongoing external awareness of technology, competitors, and emerging and disruptive opportunities.

  • Engage directly with requestors to clarify needs, define project scope, and ensure alignment of expectations.

  • Maintain clear and consistent communication with team members to support effective and efficient daily operations.

  • Recruit, onboard, and train new team members as needed to support team growth and capability development.

  • Promote a culture of safety by modeling safe work practices and identifying opportunities for continuous safety improvement.

  • Manage multiple projects in a dynamic, fast-paced environment, ensuring timely and high-quality deliverables.

  • Actively participate in the broader RD&E community to contribute to business growth and cross-functional collaboration.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Ph.D. in Materials Science, Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or related field with 4 years of relevant work experience or M.S. with 6 years of relevant work experience or B.S. with 10 years of relevant work experience.

  • Track record of successful technology development, new product / service launch and leveraging of external technology

  • Proven ability to motivate and lead individuals as part of a high-performing team.

  • Strong commitment to safety, including safe handling of chemicals and equipment, and promoting a safe work environment for colleagues and self.

  • Excellent time management skills and organizational skills.

  • Excellent written, oral, and visual communication skills to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization, both within and outside the business.

  • Proficient knowledge of MS Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Outlook.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Previous supervisory experience.

  • Experience with enhanced oil recovery methods/business.

  • Experience with Specialty Chemicals in the Oil & Gas industry.

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

job.title: Application Specialist
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

If you are a service professional diligent in time management, we invite you to apply. 

This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive pay and benefits (hourly pay).  You will be primarily responsible for working with Sales Representatives on executing service plans in strategic district accounts.   You will be required to assist District Sales Representatives with start-ups of both new applications in current customers, and in transitioning competitively held accounts to ChampionX. In addition, you must install, maintain, and calibrate chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at multiple customer locations.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.

  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.

  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 25%

  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience

  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

job.title: Engineering Manager
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Engineering Manager located in Rockyview, AB (near Calgary).

The Engineering Manager supports the execution of engineered‑to‑order fabrication projects by bridging engineering, sales, operations, supply chain, and project management.

This role is focused on front‑end engineering, technical evaluation, and feasibility assessment during the inquiry and quoting phase, while also supporting early project setup for awarded work.

The Engineering Manager plays a key role in ensuring that customer requirements, technical specifications, material availability, and shop capacity are aligned before commercial commitments are finalized, reducing execution risk and improving project predictability.

This position is intended as a developmental role that provides broad exposure to the full project lifecycle and prepares the individual for deeper technical, project, or operational leadership responsibilities over time.

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible and independent work environment that fosters a good work-life balance.

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, vision, dental, prescription drugs, travel insurance, life insurance, and more!

  • Company-paid pension plan, employee and family assistance program, paid vacation, sick and personal days.

What You Will Do:

Front‑End Engineering & Quoting Support

  • Review customer specifications, datasheets, and inquiry documentation to establish technical requirements and design basis

  • Support conceptual and preliminary design development in collaboration with Engineering

  • Prepare technical input for quotes, including:

    • Scope definition

    • Design assumptions

    • Key risks and exclusions

  • Identify technical complexity, fabrication challenges, and execution constraints early in the inquiry phase

  • Support engineering‑led pricing by providing design‑based input into material and labor assumptions

Cross‑Functional Feasibility & Risk Assessment

  • Coordinate internally with:

    • Engineering to confirm design feasibility and standards

    • Operations to validate high‑level shop loading, sequencing, and execution constraints

    • Supply Chain to identify long‑lead materials, vendor risks, and procurement constraints

  • Surface execution risks, constraints, and trade‑off options prior to finalizing delivery commitments

  • Document key assumptions and risks to enable informed commercial decision‑making

Project Handover & Early Execution Support

  • Support clean handoff of awarded projects to Project Management, including:

    • Defined scope and assumptions

    • Identified risks and mitigation strategies

    • Technical clarification of quoted configurations

  • Assist with early engineering coordination to ensure drawings, documentation, and material requirements align with execution strategy

  • Provide technical clarification during early project phases as required

Technical Communication & Continuous Improvement

  • Act as a technical point of contact for internal clarification questions during quoting and early project phases

  • Support development and refinement of standard designs, templates, and estimating inputs

  • Capture lessons learned from executed projects to improve future feasibility assessments and quoting accuracy

  • Improve clarity and consistency of internal communication between functions

Reporting Relationship

  • Reports functionally within Engineering.

  • Works closely with Sales, Project Management, Operations, and Supply Chain

  • Receives direction and prioritization in support of commercial commitments from the Site / Business Manager

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Calgary, AB or the surrounding area.

  • Work schedule: 4 days from the office and 1 day from home.

  • Full-time position, 40 hours/week.

  • Immigration sponsorship is not available for this position.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in engineering (Mechanical, Chemical, Industrial, or related discipline).

  • P.Eng required.

  • 5+ years of experience in an engineered‑to‑order, fabrication, or industrial manufacturing environment.

  • Any software or program requirements?

  • Strong technical aptitude with the ability to interpret specifications and drawings

  • Working knowledge of fabrication processes, materials, and industrial equipment

  • Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills

  • Ability to communicate technical information clearly across disciplines

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience supporting proposals, estimating, or front‑end engineering

  • Exposure to shop operations, production planning, or supply chain coordination

  • Familiarity with pressure equipment, mechanical design, or industrial process systems

  • Experience in cross‑functional or matrixed environments

Key Competencies & Attributes

  • Systems‑oriented thinker with strong technical judgment

  • Comfortable working with incomplete information and managing ambiguity

  • Able to challenge assumptions constructively and professionally

  • Organized, detail‑oriented, and disciplined in documentation

  • Respected communicator who can work effectively across functions

  • Interest in developing broad business and execution understanding, beyond pure design work

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Regional Sales Manager
job.description:

job.title: Hone Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Hone Operator - 2nd shift located in Crowley, TX. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

What You Will Do:

  • Produce barrels through utilization of saws, straighteners, CNC equipment, hones, paint equipment, and inspection processes while following standard work methods to meet customer expectations in accordance with all quality and safety protocols.

  • Learn, adhere, and apply principles of Lean Manufacturing to provide and maintain a high-quality standard

  • Learn about and participate in accomplishing Harbison-Fischer’s strategy through participation in daily team meetings

  • Participate in Continuous Improvement Program

  • Understand and operate all machines and equipment within the department

  • Cross-train on multiple pieces of equipment and processes to maximize capabilities and productivity

  • Rotate as needed among all necessary processes

  • Excellent organizational and communication skills

  • Ability to multi-task

Physical Demands:  

  • Ability to lift up to 50 pounds and standing or walking in the work area for most of the day is a requirement of the job.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands and fingers to pick, feel, and/or inspect material. The employee will also reach with arms and hands as needed.

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Experience in the oil and gas industry preferred

  • Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Office suite - Picks up on technical things quickly; can learn new skills and knowledge is good at learning new industry, company, product, or technical knowledge; does well in technical courses and seminars.

What’s In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • The chance to Improve Lives in our community with regular outreach and volunteerism opportunities

  • The chance to join a learning and problem-solving culture

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!


What You Will Do:

  • Interpret and follow blueprints, diagrams, and technical instructions for inspections.

  • Inspect materials, parts, assemblies, and sub-assemblies according  to drawings and specifications

  • Communicate quality control issues and recommend changes to enhance product quality

  • Perform routine checks on red tag bins in the production areas to ensure established NCR procedures are followed.

  • Perform incoming inspection of purchased product in accordance with industry standard and AQL sampling plan

  • Assure that manufactured product meet quality control standards, engineering specifications and customer requirements

  • Participate in investigation and disposition of customer complaints, nonconforming products and returned goods

  • Read and interpret material test reports and heat treat / mechanical certs

  • Demonstrate a personable, positive, and enthusiastic attitude

  • Report unsafe acts or conditions to supervision immediately as noticed

  • Initiate corrective action requests when conditions are averse to quality

  • Complete, inspect and distribute all quality records and documents, as necessary

  • Participate in Lean events with active involvement in CI initiatives

  • Understand and follow verbal and written instruction


Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug & Alcohol screening

  • Criminal background review

  • Education & Employment history review


Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in: Houston TX, or surrounding areas

  • Work week and shift: 6AM-2:30PM, M – F, some daily overtime and weekend work may be occasionally required

  • Compensation package offered: Competitive hourly rate and quarterly bonus eligibility

  • New hire orientation is provided

  • Training provided on processes, procedures, and product assembly

  • PPE requirements may include safety glasses, steel toed shoes, gloves, etc.

  • Work location is in an enclosed shop quality lab environment with an open workspace

  • Immigration sponsorship is not provided for this position


Minimum Qualifications:

  • 3 years’ experience in quality inspection within a manufacturing environment

  • Experience in an Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) system and Quality Management Software (QMS)

  • Must be skilled in using inspection equipment including Calipers, Mic’s, Bore gauges, Optical Comparator, Hardness Testers, Height Gage etc.

  • Self-starter, highly motivated, able to work with limited supervision

  • Must be able to read and interpret material certs and heat treat / mechanical certs

  • Knowledge of assembly inspection, testing and manufacturing

  • Knowledge of Machine Shop In-process Inspection, ECN Process and GD&T

  • Knowledge of quality standards and manufacturing processes

  • Must be able to work independently and within a team environment with a high degree of accuracy, strong organization skill as well as excellent attention to detail

  • Proficient with Microsoft Office Suite

  • Show respect, understanding, tact and concern to others; develops and maintains effective relationships with others

  • Maintain confidentiality and has high ethical standards 

  • Demonstrate a personable, positive, and enthusiastic attitude

  • Foster teamwork and collaboration within and across work groups

  • Is open to change and new information; adapts behavior or work methods in response to new information, changing conditions or unexpected obstacles; effectively deals with ambiguity

  • Must have a professional and positive attitude as well as a passion for continuous improvement

  • Understanding of current process requirements, rules, and procedures

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Minimal bending, crouching, kneeling, squatting, climbing stairs, reaching, twisting, stretching, pushing, pulling, collating/filing, and dialing.

  • Occasional standing and hearing.

  • Frequent lifting between 25lbs to 40lbs, walking and writing.

  • Constant lifting up to 10lbs, handling, holding, grasping, turning, working with hands, fingering, picking, pinching, fine manipulation, using a keyboard and mouse, viewing a computer monitor and small parts and products, sitting, seeing and speaking.

  • Work environment includes temperature-controlled shop floor, quality lab, front offices and conference rooms.

    Preferred Qualifications:

    • Experience in Oracle ERP

    • Experience in Entropy QMS

    • Experience in Adobe

    • Knowledgeable of Industry Standard Requirements (ANSI, ASME, ISO 9001, etc.)

    • Operate and program CMM (knowledge or FARO/ROMER arm a plus)

    About Us:

    ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

job.title: Instrumentation & Electrical Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Instrumentation & Electrical Technician at our Freeport, Texas, manufacturing facility. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing organization that values safety, continuous improvement, and operational excellence.

What's in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including top-notch safety training and personal protective equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity to work with a collaborative team focused on reliability, continuous improvement, and operational excellence
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching, and more

What You Will Do:

Read and correct the following drawings:

  • Piping and Instrument Drawings (P&ID)
  • Electrical Drawings
  • Instrument Loop Drawings
  • Schematics
  • Troubleshoot, repair, and calibrate all plant instrumentation and electrical equipment as necessary.
  • Able to install, repair, and complete revisions to plant instrumentation and electrical equipment while following drawings and making "As Built" corrections or drawings as needed.
  • After being trained in the use of the DocuMint Database and Calibrations, will use them for scheduling, calibration, and documentation of plant instrumentation.
  • Maintains the instrumentation and electrical spare parts inventory.
  • Performs all work while emphasizing safety and maintaining a clean, safe, and environmentally compliant work area.
  • Supports the guiding principles of Responsible Care to continually improve Freeport plant's safety, health and environmental performance, security, and efforts to respond to public concerns relating to design, development, manufacture, storage, marketing, distribution, use and ultimate safe disposal of chemical products.
  • Supports and is committed to the Quality Improvement Process. Plans strategies to ensure error-free work by "doing it right the first time" and conveys this attitude in the daily performance of various duties.
  • Supports and incorporates the activities of Continuous Improvement into ongoing work duties through teamwork within and across departments. Uses Continuous Improvement to eliminate losses and waste that hamper facility effectiveness so that safety is advanced, process and equipment are reliable, quality is stabilized/improved, and operating costs are reduced.

Position Details:

  • Plant located in: Freeport, Texas
  • Work week and shift: Regular hours Monday through Friday, with the exception of a weekend rotational on-call responsibility

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma/GED
  • 2 years of experience in I/E in an industrial maintenance environment
  • Good analytical skills and attention to detail with ability to read and interpret blueprints, plans, and manuals
  • Must have the ability to don a respirator
  • Must have the ability to wear fall protection if necessary
  • Must be able to work in confined spaces and be capable of working at elevation, including from a manlift or ladder
  • No immigration sponsorship offered

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to bend, squat down, and reach above your head
  • Ability to climb stairs and ladders
  • Able to lift and carry up to 50 pounds
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Formal vocational training or certification in a plant maintenance area of expertise
  • Forklift operation certified
  • Ability to work independently with minimal direct supervision
  • Prior chemical plant maintenance experience

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that has been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative changemakers are focused on advancing innovation and inventing the new energy technologies needed to get there.

job.title: Supply Planner
job.description:
Disclaimer

SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for a Supply Planner I located in Sugar Land, Texas. Experienced professionals to support our Supply Chain Planning Team within Chemical Technologies. This is an opportunity to join a growing global organization offering strong career development, resources, and benefits.

What’s in it for You

  • Access to best-in-class tools, technology, and continuous learning opportunities
  • A strong safety culture with industry-leading training and personal protective equipment
  • Opportunity to work for a company focused on clean water, safe food, and sustainable energy
  • Company vehicle, fuel card, and cell phone provided (if applicable)
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company match

Key Responsibilities

  • Conversion of demand plan into a network supply plan for Family Chemistries.  This includes approving and sending a production plan to manufacturing locations while optimizing network capacity.
  • Identify and resolve forecast accuracy and lead time issues with key stakeholders including Demand Planning, SBUs, and PLMs
  • Balance production planning across the network to maintain sprint capacity, including load leveling in preparation for maintenance shutdowns, seasonal volumes, raw material allocation etc.
  • Identify and resolve capacity constraints over the planning horizon by working with Manufacturing, R&D, Procurement, and the Business Units. 
  • Maintain required inventory levels for Family Chemistries at stocking locations to meet customer service levels.  Implement corrective action when imbalances occur.
  • Regularly monitor and address inactive, slow moving, and excess inventory
  • Coordinate inter-plant scheduling constraints related to schedule break-ins, raw materials, etc. facilitated through regular communication.
  • Lead and implement improvement plans to ensure security of supply for existing and new business
  • Conduct root cause analysis for supply chain issues/constraints/process gaps and drive corrective actions

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in business, Supply Chain Management, Data Science, or a related subject.
  • Minimum 2 years of relevant experience.
  • Authorization to work in the U.S. without sponsorship
  • High level proficiency with MS Office, primarily Excel
  • Excellent communication, organization, and time management skills

Preferred Qualifications

  • APICS Certification
  • High level of proficiency with common ERP systems, especially JDA Master Planning and SAP (Materials Management (MM), Production Planning (PP))
  • A track record of successfully delivering to Key Performance Indicators and business objectives

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. For nearly a century, we have focused on unlocking access to energy while advancing sustainability.

Our teams are committed to developing innovative technologies that support a future with more energy and less carbon.

For more information, visit SLB.com
Follow us on Facebook (@WeAreSLBGlobal), LinkedIn (SLBGlobal), and Instagram (@SLBGlobal)

job.title: Assistant Plant Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer:

SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has future and immediate hiring needs for experienced Manufacturing Blend Operator I (Plant Operator I) professionals in Scott, LA to support our operations team. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering competitive pay, career growth opportunities, and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity for career advancement within a global organization
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching, and more

Summary

Responsible for assisting in the safe and efficient operations of plant operations. This includes all aspects of producing, manufacturing, blending, storing, and handling product. Maintains the highest standards of safety, quality, housekeeping, and environmental awareness at all times. Responsible for proper documentation and communication of important information to leadership. This role is safety-sensitive.

What You Will Do:

  • Complete most work functions within production operations
  • Complete general forklift duties and operate yard truck
  • Safely and reliably collect samples and transfer bulk raw materials to and from tankers and storage tanks
  • Safely and reliably package finished products to and from pails, drums, IBCs, SS totes, tankers, and bulk storage tanks
  • Operate mechanical and electrical equipment such as pumps, mixing equipment, filters, and automated control systems used in packaging products
  • Complete and comply with all safety initiatives and certification requirements, including all near miss and incident reporting
  • Be accountable for the data entry of production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation
  • Ensure a clean and organized work environment is always maintained
  • Assist with physical inventory counts and processes
  • Comply with all applicable ISO requirements

Position Details:

  • Location: Scott, LA
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing
  • Ability to work in a manufacturing and plant environment
  • Ability to safely operate forklifts and other plant equipment
  • May require overtime, weekends, or shift work based on operational needs

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • 6 months to 3 years of relevant manufacturing, production, blending, warehouse, or plant operations experience
  • Ability to follow safety procedures and operational guidelines
  • Basic computer and documentation skills
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to lift/push/pull/carry up to 55 pounds
  • Ability to stand, walk, bend, climb, and work in a manufacturing environment for extended periods
  • Ability to wear required PPE and comply with all safety requirements

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Prior experience in chemical manufacturing or plant operations
  • Forklift certification or prior forklift operating experience
  • Experience working with pumps, blending systems, filters, or automated control systems
  • Experience with inventory processes and production documentation
  • Strong communication, teamwork, and organizational skills

About Us

job.title: Shipping/Receiving Coordinator (Temporary)
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Temporary Shipping & Receiving Coordinator for our Rocky View, AB location.

The Shipping & Receiving Coordinator is responsible for receiving, inspecting, organizing, and shipping materials and fabricated equipment in a manufacturing/fabrication environment. This role requires familiarity with fabrication materials, the ability to review Material Test Reports (MTRs), strong organizational skills, and the ability to perform accurate transactions in SAP.

This is a temporary 1-year contract

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world

What Will You Do:

  • Receive incoming materials and fabricated components, verifying quantities, condition, and documentation against purchase orders (POs).

  • Coordinate outbound shipments, including packaging, labeling, and scheduling with carriers.

  • Prepare standard shipping documentation such as packing lists, bills of lading, and USMCA documentation

  • Maintain organized warehouse, yard, and staging areas to support fabrication and shipment flow.

  • Review and verify Material Test Reports (MTRs) for completeness and alignment with received materials.

  • Confirm material traceability including heat numbers and material grades.

  • Work with QC and fabrication teams to resolve discrepancies related to materials or documentation.

  • Perform goods receipt, inventory movements, and shipment transactions in SAP.

  • Ensure all materials are received against the correct purchase orders and projects.

  • Track expected PO arrivals and communicate issues related to late, early, or incomplete deliveries.

  • Maintain accurate inventory records and support cycle counts as required.

  • Follow all site safety procedures related to material handling, lifting, and forklift operation.

  • Maintain a clean, safe, and orderly shipping and receiving area.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent.

  • 3-7 years of experience in shipping and receiving within a fabrication or manufacturing environment. Familiarity with fabrication materials such as steel, pipe, and industrial components.

  • Ability to review and understand Material Test Reports (MTRs).

  • Proficiency with SAP for receipts, inventory, and PO management.

  • Strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

  • Comfortable working in a shop environment.

  • Strong and clear communication skills.

  • Takes ownership of documentation and material accuracy.

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Calgary or Rocky View, AB or the surrounding area.

  • This position is Monday to Friday on-site, based on 40 hours per week.

  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role.

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to push, pull, lift and carry up to 50lbs

  • This role is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete pre-employment testing in accordance with company policy.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Forklift certification or willingness to obtain.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. job.title: Class 1 Truck Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Truck Driver located in Leduc, AB.  The Truck Driver is responsible for providing customer delivery of bulk chemicals throughout British Columbia, Alberta and Saskatchewan.  

The Specialist safely operates multiple configuration bulk trucks over congested city highways, rolling and mountainous terrain and rural roads. The Specialist also provides preventative maintenance of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and warehouse locations.

If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-paid pension plan and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Operates vehicle in a safe and environmentally responsible manner and maintains accurate and complete records pertaining to the transportation of dangerous goods

  • Operates the transport unit business to maintain and improve profitability by managing all aspects of operational costs

  • Assists in developing operating systems and procedures which reduce costs, maintain quality and ensure safe product delivery

  • Conducts daily pre and post-trip inspections in order to maintain the condition and safe operation of the truck and equipment

  • Verifies and confirms trucking documentation is completed in accordance with established company and regulatory requirements

  • Confirms product placement and volumes are correct according to documentation including load sheets, bills of lading and trip log

  • Completes all training and maintains appropriate certification and licensing as required by law for the transportation of dangerous goods

  • Adheres to and maintains all applicable company, legal and regulatory requirements

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Leduc, AB or the surrounding area

  • Position working 60 hours per week.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered for this role

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School diploma, GED

  • Valid Class 1 driver's license

  • Acceptable Motor Vehicle Record (5 years)

  • 5+ years of oilfield or dangerous goods hauling experience

Physical Demand:

  • This is a safety-sensitive position, and the successful candidate will be required to complete drug and alcohol testing.

  • This role requires individual to be clean shaven to comply with customer site policy and safety requirement

  • Must be able to lift and carry a minimum of 50lbs

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Certification in WHMIS, TDG, First Aid and H2S

  • Experience operating forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauge

  • Must possess strong reading comprehension, writing and mathematical abilities

  • Must possess a strong safety focus and demonstrated history of safe practices

  • Experience handling products in various types of packages including bulk and drums

  • Ability to back straight trucks, semi-trucks and trailers into narrow passages through expensive industrial equipment

  • Experience with transportation of bulk liquids

  • Knowledge of equipment, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and Transport Canada regulations governing the transportation of hazardous materials

job.title: Supply Chain Intern
job.description:
Estamos buscando Estagiário em Supply Chain, para atuar em Macaé/RJ. Se você é um estudante de graduação motivado e ansioso para aprender, convidamos você a se inscrever nessa oportunidade.

 

O que temos para você:

 

• A oportunidade de construir uma base sólida para sua carreira com uma organização que enfatiza o desenvolvimento de talentos.

• A oportunidade de causar impacto por meio de um estágio baseado em projeto, ao mesmo tempo em que ganha exposição à alta liderança.

  • Conhecimentos gerais no pacote office 365;

  • Disponibilidade para estagiar por 2 anos.

job.title: Chemical Blending Operator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Chemical Blending Operator located in Leduc, AB.  If you are a dedicated Production Operator with a proven track record of success, we invite you to apply.  The Blending Operator will be primarily responsible for carrying out the assigned plant production schedules in order to meet internal and external customer requirements. 

What’s in it For You:

  • A flexible, independent work environment

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Grow your income as you drive growth

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a company-paid pension plan, tuition reimbursement and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Progress through the Operator Progression Program within the warehouse, yard and blend floor

  • Ensure the safe and efficient receipt, storage and dispatch of warehouse materials

  • Assist with physical inventory counts and processes

  • Prepare and perform chemical transfers to and from storage tanks, trucks and railcars

  • Safely and reliably blend quality finished products and package bulk, drum and IBC products

  • Enter production tickets, goods receipts, and shipping documentation in a timely manner

  • Inspect and clean the work area daily

  • Perform required routine operator maintenance

  • Comply with all applicable ISO procedures

Position Details:

  • Candidate must reside in Leduc, AB or the surrounding area.

  • Working hours are Monday-Friday, starting at 6 AM.

  • Ability to work overtime when required.

  • Immigration Sponsorship is not available for this role

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or equivalent.

Physical Demands:

  • This role is a safety-sensitive position and the successful candidate will be required to complete pre-employment testing in accordance with ChampionX’s policy

  • Ability to push, pull, lift and carry up to 50lbs

  • The physical environment requires the employee to work both inside and outside in heat/cold, wet/humid and dry/arid conditions.

  • Must be clean-shaven upon request, due to safety situations

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Dedicated to safety and quality

  • Forklift experience

  • Rail yard experience, including unloading chemical cars

  • Previous experience with ChampionX as a Blending Operator preferred

  • Chemical manufacturing experience or equivalent industry experience

  • SAP knowledge and experience

  • Demonstrates initiative for self-improvement and assuming additional responsibilities

  • Knowledge of automated control systems

  • Responsible and flexible team player with a positive, “can-do” attitude

  • Intermediate level computer skills

job.title: Oilfield Chemical Sales Representative
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have immediate and future hiring needs for a Chemical Sales Representative in Midland, TX. If you are a passionate sales professional with a proven track record of success in technical sales, we invite you to apply. This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary, bonus structure and benefits.

You will be primarily responsible for working with Sales Representatives on executing service plans in strategic district accounts.   You will be required to assist District Sales Representatives with start-ups of both new applications in current customers, and in transitioning competitively-held accounts to ChampionX In addition, you must install, maintain, and calibrate chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at multiple customer locations.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Work closely with current and new customers to understand business needs and recommend continuous improvement and innovation plans that will maintain and grow sales within assigned territory

  • Assist in performing customer service and account management activities under the guidance and supervision of experienced sales professionals

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer complaints, escalating as required

  • Engage in problem solving by performing system analysis, interpreting data and providing written recommendations to ensure customer operations are performing at optimal levels.

  • Actively sell and support ChampionX innovations and technology in assigned customers to promote long-term business relationships with ChampionX

  • Assist in developing strong relationships with key stakeholders in current and new customers, including plant or facility executives

  • Achieve annual sales target by working collaboratively on targeting new business opportunities through application of professional selling and planning techniques

  • Assist with collection of samples and conduct laboratory analysis

Position Details:

  • Up to 25% overnight travel required to support sales territory

  • Ability to travel within assigned sales territory

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or Equivalent

  • 2 years of experience of upstream production chemical experience in a successful technical sales or field sales support position

  • Must have a valid Driver’s License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role  

Physical Demands:

  • May be required to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Industrial), or Life Sciences (Biology, Chemistry, etc.)

  • 3 or more years of experience of upstream production chemical experience in a successful technical sales or field sales support position

  • Prior experience in the oil and gas industry

  • High computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that required excellent communication and organizational skills

  • Prior experience that demonstrates strong ethics, effective time management, ability to multitask, adaptability, autonomy and self-motivation

  • Mechanically Inclined

job.title: Junior Plant Operator/Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. 

 

We are looking for a Junior Plant Operator/Driver located in Lloydminster, AB. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. 

 

What’s In It For You:  

The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best 

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement 

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-matched retirement program, and more! 

 

What You Will Do:  

  • Responsible for the assembly, inventory and inspection of all products 

  • Operate overhead crane as needed 

  • Operate a three-axle rod hauling truck as needed 

  • Operate a field fusion welding truck as needed 

  • Loading of full transport reels and unloading of empty transport reels onto/off of transport trailers 

  • Communicate with the Edmonton facility regarding inventory levels 

  • Stamp Pro-Rod accessories  

  • Assist Senior Operators in the maintenance of equipment 

  • Housekeeping duties 

  • Fill out appropriate paperwork 

  • Assist in monthly and quarterly inventory counts 

  • Other duties as required 

 

Pre-Employment Requirements:  

  • Drug and alcohol testing 

  • Fitness-to-work testing 

  • Drivers’ abstract  

 

Position Details:  

  • Permanent 

  • Full-time 

  • Mainly working day-shift operations, with overtime as required 

  • Weekend or night shift may be required dependent on customer needs 

  • Travel to customer sites 

 

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • Minimum 2 years’ experience working with mechanical assembly and service 

  • Experience working with machinery and power tools  

 

Preferred Qualifications:  

  • Valid class 3 driver’s license with a clean record 

  • Valid class 1 driver's license with clean record 

  • Ability to read blueprints

job.title: Cable Repair Technician II
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Cable Repair Technician II - Night Shift located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Perform moderately complex cable builds and repairs for ESP cable
  • Perform High voltage testing of ESP cable
  • Perform splicing to ESP cable
  • Perform repairs to ESP cable
  • Perform spooling of ESP cable
  • Able to complete required testing documentation
  • Work schedule is 7:00 p.m. – 7:00 a.m. on a rotating basis

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 1 years of experience in ESP experience
  • Basic troubleshooting skills for electrical equipment
  • Basic computer skills with programs like Microsoft Word and Excel
  • Ability to read and write English
  • Accuracy and Attention to detail
  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers
  • Ability to work under general supervision
  • Effectively present information and respond to questions
  • Intermediate verbal and written communication skills
  • Action-oriented for delivering results
  • Customer-first mindset
  • Successful planning, priority setting, informing, results, and time management skills.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 
  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.
  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up-to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up-to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.
  • Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.
  • Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.
  • Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.
  • Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.
  • Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.
  • Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.
  • Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.
  • Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.
  • Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.
  • Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.
  • Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.
  • Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.
  • Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.
  • Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.
  • Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.
  • Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.
  • Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.
  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.
  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.
  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.
  • Indoor and outdoor environmental conditions.  Activities occur indoors and outdoors.
  • Extreme heat.  Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour.  Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity.
  • Hazards.  Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals.
  • Atmospheric conditions.  One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation.
  • Oils. There is air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

job.title: Shop Operator I 2nd shift
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Shop Technician 2nd shift located in Broken Arrow, OK supporting our Electric Submersible Pump (ESP) business.This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • Second shift generally starts at 3:15 pm and may be 8, 10 or 12 hours depending on the workload (with some weekend overtime as needed)

  • $20+ per hour based on prior experience with a $2.00 per hour shift differential

  • Thrive in a company that values safety and continuous improvement and encourages career growth

  • Excellent benefits, including bonus potential, 11 paid holidays, generous vacation, personal time, medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company match, disability, life insurance, tuition assistance, paid parental leave, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Assemble and repair products such as pumps and motors, following defined work instructions, and using hand and power tools in a manufacturing environment

  • Some minor welding (if qualified)

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • This safety-sensitive role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • Read, understand and speak English

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

  • Must be willing to work overtime and Saturdays as required

Pre-Employment requirements

  • Drug screen

  • Background Check

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Medium work:  Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Requires Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as safety shoes and safety glasses

  • May stand, walk, stoop, sit, bend, twist, grasp, lift, and climb

  • Requires visual and hearing acuity

  • Work primarily occurs in a heated and air-conditioned manufacturing environment

  • Hazards include proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals

  • Atmospheric conditions that affect the respiratory system or skin may include fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation

  • Potential skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids

  • Must be willing to work overtime and Saturdays as required

job.title: Software Engineer
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

Artificial Lift Performance Ltd (ALP) is seeking an ambitious Software Engineer located in Edinburgh, UK. The Software Engineer will be responsible for delivering high-quality commercial software, mentor other developers and be ensure that the platform support is commercially focused and that the engineering team are fulfilling their 3rd line support responsibilities.

What’s in it For You:

The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

The support of an organisation that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

Comprehensive benefits package & flexible working arrangements that enable balancing work commitments with other priorities

What You Will Do:

As an engineer:

Key to this appointment is a strong sense of software ownership and the desire to support and develop junior members of the team. This person must have a strong engineering background, successful track record in delivering high-quality commercial software and can establish credibility as well as providing input into technical decision making. Being able to organise your own work, problem solve efficiently and know when to consult are necessary traits to succeed in this position

As a technical leader:

  • Influence and make good technical design decisions.
  • Actively contribute to technical and architectural discussions.
  • Own feature brief documents for product enhancements.
  • Participate in the evolution of the architecture to support the product road map, and play an instrumental role in the software engineering team
  • Deal with technical challenges that are particularly high profile, urgent, or strategically important to get right.
  • Have a pedigree of delivering successful technology solutions, across multiple products.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 2 or 3+ years relevant experience.
  • Candidates should be passionate about quality software development and have a strong desire to learn and improve.
  • Good problem-solving skills, and the ability to work independently and in a team are essential.
  • Primary technical knowledge and background in C# (with an interest and experience in other languages), Entity Framework and ASP.NET MVC.
  • Knowledge and understanding of Object-Oriented Programming principles and design.
  • Experience with relational and non-relational data technologies bringing a breadth of knowledge and experience across this domain.
  • Experience in analysing and tuning database technologies to performance at scale
  • Experience in front-end technologies such as HTML5, CSS and JavaScript.
  • Knowledge or experience of unit and integration testing frameworks.

About Our Stack:

We operate across several innovative technology domains, including cloud computing, data analytics, machine learning, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Our current stack features ASP.NET MVC using .NET 8, Vue 3 with TypeScript, Kendo UI, Highcharts, Google Cloud Platform (GCP), and SQL Server, with opportunities to engage with technologies such as Python, HTMX, Blazor, PostgreSQL, SCSS and Azure Dev ops.

Looking ahead, we’re undertaking a major re-architecture of our platform to adopt a microservices-based, containerized approach—enhancing scalability and flexibility, and enabling integration with software systems in our wider organisation. As we evolve our platform, we’re leveraging GCP and Azure to build robust data pipelines and consumption layers that support advanced analytics and machine learning capabilities.

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Chemist
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

This Chemist position applies foundational chemical knowledge to perform laboratory testing, analyze data, and support research and operational objectives, including collecting samples in the field as needed. The role focuses on executing established methods, ensuring accuracy and efficiency, and maintaining high standards in safety and quality while managing a high-volume workload.

Working within a collaborative team environment and reporting to the Regional Lab Manager, this individual contributes to effective lab operations through strong communication, accountability, and teamwork. The ideal candidate brings 1–5 years of laboratory experience, solid analytical skills, and the ability to manage responsibilities independently while supporting a safe, efficient, and team-oriented laboratory culture.

Minimum Education & Experience Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 2 years of US Land oilfield production chemistry experience, or
  • Master's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 1 years of US Land oilfield production chemistry experience
  • Demonstrated experience supporting production chemistry programs for U.S. land-based oil and gas operations.
  • Experience managing high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, and turnaround time expectations.
  • Proven ability to follow SOPs, work independently, prioritize competing demands, and manage moderately complex projects with minimal supervision.
  • Experience operating and troubleshooting ICP-OES, Ion Chromatography (IC), Gas Chromatography (GC), XRF, or equivalent analytical instrumentation.
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, etc) and Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), with experience leveraging data management and reporting tools to support laboratory operations, decision-making, and process improvement.

 

Required Skills & Experience (Must – Haves) 

  • Demonstrated success working within a laboratory team where collaboration, flexibility, and individual accountability are critical to overall laboratory performance.
  • Ability and willingness to perform field sampling activities, including traveling to client or site locations, safely collecting environmental or process samples in various conditions, and adhering to all field safety protocols and regulatory requirements.

  • Demonstrate initiative and ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks.
  • Demonstrated ability to adapt to changing priorities while maintaining a strong commitment to safety, quality, and customer responsiveness.
  • Demonstrated desire and initiative to grow into leadership roles over time, including taking on increased responsibility, mentoring peers, and contributing to team development.
  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail.
  • Effective verbal and written communication skills with the ability to communicate technical information to both technical and non-technical audiences.
  • Physically able to lift 50 pounds, climb stairs or ladder, and agile enough to squat and bend over.
  • Ability to independently prioritize tasks, manage multiple projects, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.
  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage a high-volume workload (handling up to 1,000 samples per month).

Preferred Skills & Experience (Nice to Have)

  • 3-7 years of experience in oilfield production chemistry, chemical manufacturing, or industrial analytical laboratory environments.
  •  3+ years of laboratory leadership, technical leadership, mentoring, or senior laboratory experience supporting production chemistry operations.
  • Demonstrated history of taking ownership of laboratory responsibilities, following through on commitments, and consistently delivering high-quality results.
  • Proven ability to foster a collaborative, respectful, and solutions-oriented work environment through effective communication and constructive engagement with colleagues and stakeholders.
  • Experience presenting technical findings, managing projects, and collaborating effectively across functional teams.
  • Understanding of customer needs and ability to translate analytical data into actionable operational insights.
  • Experience identifying and implementing process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, or customer service.
  • Experience with Six Sigma, Lean, or continuous improvement methodologies in a manufacturing or factory setting.

Job Duties:

  • Lead and execute routine and non-routine analytical testing activities in support of customer programs, field operations, and laboratory service commitments.
  • Independently manage multiple priorities, project deadlines, customer requests, and stakeholder communications in a fast-paced laboratory environment.
  • Manage high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, data integrity, and turnaround time expectations.
  • Operate, maintain, troubleshoot, and optimize laboratory instrumentation while ensuring compliance with established QA/QC procedures and laboratory standards.
  • Support team training efforts by learning procedures, sharing information when appropriate, and assisting colleagues as needed.
  • Assist with compiling and communicating analytical data to help support team recommendations and customer needs..
  • Identify opportunities for process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, and customer service.
  • Champion safety culture, support continuous improvement initiatives, and contribute positively to overall laboratory performance, efficiency, and team effectiveness.

Salary: $70,000 - $80,000

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal.

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Journeyperson Millwright
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Journeyperson Millwright located in Edmonton, AB. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It for You:

The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture, and empowers through continuous improvement
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes extended health benefits, a company-matched retirement program, and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Organize needed materials for the task
  • Perform mechanical repairs and rebuilds, including but not limited to gearboxes, belt and mechanical driven conveyor systems, diaphragm pumping systems, pneumatics components and tools, and properly align to tolerance bearings, gears, shafts and couplings
  • Install new and used machinery and equipment
  • Move existing equipment within the building
  • Complete welding, basic electrical, plumbing and fabricating as per building requirements
  • Contact with outside contractors
  • Complete associated paperwork, including dispatch requests, PM’s, etc…
  • Supervision of Apprentice Millwrights may be required
  • Prioritization of dispatch requests
  • Perform rigging and operation of cranes and forklifts
  • Training of employees
  • Delegation of tasks
  • Follow Lean manufacturing guidelines
  • Work in a safe manner, follow all rules, regulations and legislation
  • Manage and maintain a clean and organized work environment

Pre-Employment Requirements:

  • Drug and alcohol testing
  • Fitness-to-work testing
  • Background check

Position Details:

  • Working at the Edmonton manufacturing facility
  • Permanent
  • Full-time
  • Rotating shift between day and afternoon shift (night shift may be required dependent on business conditions)
  • Weekend or night shift may be required dependent on business conditions

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Journeyperson ticket
  • Able to read blueprints
  • Ability to supervise and multi-task
  • Ability to prioritize and problem solve
  • Verbal and written communication skills
  • Flexibility to work between two South Edmonton facilities, if required
  • Ability to work with minimal supervision
  • Reliable

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Red seal certification
  • Knowledge in Lean Manufacturing Principles and Total Productive Maintenance
  • Knowledge in a variety of trades is extremely beneficial
  • Programmable Logic Controls (PLC) and pneumatics experience
  • Knowledge and experience in welding (OAW, GMAW, MIG, SMAW)
  • Intermediate computer skills (Microsoft office, word, and access)

job.title: Field Service Tech Assistant
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Tech Assistant to support the Licensed Electrician for the San Antonio area. This role offers the opportunity to grow into a Licensed Electrician/Journeyman career path.

This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:  

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits 

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology 

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path 

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection 

What You Will Do: 

  • Works under direct supervision to perform basic customer technical product support activities.

  • Assists higher level licensed technicians with on-site installations, check-out, and emergency repairs.

  • Refers more complex problems to higher level technicians or supervisor.  

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 2 years related field work experience 

  • Basic knowledge of industrial safety 

  • Basic knowledge of proper use of hand tools 

  • Basic knowledge of wiring and cabling 

  • Analytical and problem-solving ability 

  • Intermediate oral and written communication skills 

  • Organization and time management skills 

  • Basic math skills 

  • Ability to follow prescribed company policies, procedures, and protocols for installation, service and equipment maintenance 

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and/or coworkers 

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively 

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs 

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently 

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data 

  • Determine the priorities, goals, and objectives of work assigned 

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role 

Physical Demands:

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing. 

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 

  • Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects. 

  • Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles, and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized. 

  • Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces. 

  • Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles. 

  • Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees. 

  • Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine. 

  • Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet. 

  • Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction. 

  • Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another. 

  • Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward. 

  • Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion. 

  • Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position. 

  • Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling. 

  • Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm. 

  • Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips. 

  • Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly. 

  • Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound. 

  • Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer. 

Preferred Qualifications: 

  • Experience working with VFD’s and transformers
