job.title: Quality Analyst II
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Quality Analyst  in Scott, LA to support our manufacturing operations. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing organization that values safety, quality, continuous improvement, and operational excellence.

The Quality Analyst is primarily responsible for performing quality determination tests on production and packaging event samples and analytical analysis on all field samples.

POSITION REQUIREMENTS

Must have background in scientific skills such as, oil laboratory analysis, quality assurance and control, analytical chemistry, etc. Either a Bachelor's Degree in a science medium or 2 years equivalent experience must be provided.

PRINCIPAL ACTIVITIES

  • Responsible for performing all quality assurance tests on Raw Materials and finished products in a precise and accurate manner.
  • Inputs data into SAP for material batch test results and interfaces with Sugar Land QA department with regard to Master Inspection Characteristics, shelf life, and other sections of the Quality Module.
  • Maintains inventory status of reagents, lab equipment, etc..
  • Prepares lab standard test reagents. performs standardization on reagents and calibration of equipment on a regular basis. Keeps detailed records. Maintains control charts
  • Oversees or performs the maintenance of laboratory and/or facility analytical equipment and initiates the purchase, installation and implementation for improved monitoring or analysis. Meet requirements of ISO management system.
  • Coordinates with the Production Leader to pull samples of any Raw Materials, RMAs, Restricted Batches or QI Batches, so the Quality Analyst can perform the appropriate QA tests and analysis.
  • Use good laboratory practices to accurately record and file all raw test data, including pertinent paperwork
  • Communicates results to Production and Material Control, calculates and recommends batch adjustments to Production.
  • Maintain log Of problem batches With the action that was taken
  • Packages and ships samples to other facilities and customers. Maintains a dean, safe and orderly work environment and assures smooth operation of the QA Laboratory
  • Ensures product formulas and specifications are current and that all materials have MSDS information available.
  • Is the point person for ISO 9001 audits.
  • performs all basic calibration on lab equipment Continues improving expertise in chemistry, analytical techniques, and equipment maintenance Manages retain database and rework Of retains.
  • Assists with ECAP and other relevant audits
  • Insures that the lab is adequately covered during vacation and sick days
  • Supports the guiding principles of ISO 9001, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 to continually improve Scott plant's safety, health and environmental performance, security, and efforts to respond to public concerns relating to design, development, manufacture, storage, marketing, distribution, use and ultimate safe disposal of chemical products.
  • Supports and is committed to the Quality Improvement process. plans strategies to ensure errorfree work by "doing it right the first time" and conveys this attitude in the daily performance of various duties

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. job.title: Manufacturing Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of Champion X on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with Champion X and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Manufacturing Technician located in Broken Arrow, OK. This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:  

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth. 

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best. 

  • Thrive in a company that values safety and continuous improvement and encourages career growth. 

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:  

  • Perform technical tasks under the direction of an engineer or manager. 

  • Help support engineering project fabrication, machining, and assembly.

  • Troubleshoot and repair production equipment of various types.

  • Interact regularly with vendors and co-workers.

  • Document assembly steps and maintain records for company and customers.

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements.

  • Read and follow written and/or verbal directions and use a computer to communicate, create, and access information.

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • This safety-sensitive role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

  • One year experience with the following:

  • Performing mechanical assembly work

  • Operation of manual lathe/mill

  • Welding (GMAW, SMAW, and GTAW)

  • Ability to interpret machine drawings and electrical schematics

  • Basic understanding of single and 3 phase electrical.

  • Working knowledge of hand & power tools, overhead crane

  • Speak, read, and write English fluently.

  • Self-starter with strong organizational skills and attention to detail.

  • Ability to work overtime and Saturdays as required

  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Pre-Employment requirements 

  • Drug screen 

  • Background Check 

  • Motor Vehicle Record (MVR) check 

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 

  • Potential travel: up to 20% (domestically) 

  • Medium work: Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects. 

  • Requires Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) such as safety shoes, safety glasses, hardhats hearing protection and respirators. 

  • May stand, walk, stoop, sit, bend, twist, grasp, lift, climb,   

  • Requires visual and hearing acuity  

  • Activities occur indoors and outdoors. 

  • Extreme heat: Temperatures above 100 degrees for periods of more than one hour. Consideration should be given to the effect of other environmental conditions, such as wind and humidity. 

  • Hazards: Includes a variety of physical conditions, such as proximity to moving mechanical parts, moving vehicles, electrical current or exposure to chemicals. 

  • Atmospheric conditions: One or more of the following conditions that affect the respiratory system or the skin:  fumes, odors, dust, mists, gases, or poor ventilation. 

  • Oils: potential air and/or skin exposure to oils and other cutting fluids.

job.title: Field Service Technician (Hydraulics)
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Field Service Technician located in Laurel, MS. The Field Service Technician will be responsible for performing basic to advanced maintenance, new equipment installations, and troubleshooting actions either in the shop or field. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

  • Comprehensive benefits packages that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Executes the commissioning and decommissioning of multiplex pump skids and components at oil and gas well sites for use on Jet Pump artificial lift, Salt Water Disposal (SWD) Systems, and other facilities.

  • Perform repairs and troubleshoot multiplex pumps and it’s auxiliary components for operation.  Including but not limited to; electric motor and their variable speed drives (VSD), safety switches, flow meters, various valves, lubrication, and sump pumps.

  • Complete preventative maintenance on the above equipment at regular intervals.

  • Timely and accurately write up service tickets for the customers to review and be invoiced.

  • Develops good working relationships with customers through professional communication.  Respond to customer calls and assist with technical questions.

  • Troubleshoot, repair, and service multiplex pumps, commonly known as Triplex’s and Quintuplex’s.

  • Troubleshoot, repair, and service Jet Pump systems used for artificial lift in the oil and gas industry.

  • Responds to customer calls and assist with technical questions.

  • Participate with on-call rotation for the facility, as required by the business.

  • Maintains a clean and well-organized work area and company truck.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 2 years of experience as a field service mechanic troubleshooting and repairing multiplex pumps and jet pump artificial lift systems.

  • Must have an acceptable driving record.

Physical Demands:

  • The role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Must be able to lift and carry 25 lbs regularly and 50 lbs occasionally.

  • Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.

  • The employee is regularly required to talk or hear.

  • The employee is frequently required to use hands to finger, handle, or feel and reach with hands and arms.

  • Employee is required to occasionally walk-in customer field locations which may include inclines, uneven surfaces, and a variety of weather conditions.

  • Employee must be able to regularly bend, stoop, crawl, lay down, twist and push.

  • Employee is required to wear appropriate personal protective equipment including but not limited to shoes/boots, hard hats, or respirators.

  • Work normally is executed at customer field locations in a variety of weather and temperature conditions.

  • Some work is accomplished indoors and in air-conditioned or well-ventilated facilities.

  • Work may include visits to customer offices or field locations.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Basic understanding of downhole pumps and completions.

  • Working knowledge of multi-cylinder engines.

  • I&E (Instrumentation & Electrical) and Automation experience.

job.title: Billing Support Specialist
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration. 

We have an immediate need for a Billing Support Specialist located in Williston, ND. The Billing Support Specialist will work within our industry leading Institutional division, will play a key role providing office management support for two corporate account managers. This position is responsible for managing and prioritizing requests from these associates and ensures all necessary reporting and correspondence is completed in a timely, accurate and professional manner.  As a critical role for the business, this position expeditiously and effectively resolves the issues and concerns of large, multi-unit corporate account customers. 

If you are a passionate professional that likes to work in an autonomous, hands-on environment, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth 
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives and ideas to achieve our best 
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and has been recognized as one of the most ethical companies in the world 
  • Comprehensive benefits package starting day 1 of employment- medical, dental, vision, matching 401k, and more! 

 

What You Will Do: 

  • Responsible for Order to Cash processing including sales order entry, delivery creation, and invoicing 
  • Monitoring of accounts payable and accounts receivable 
  • Responsible for the administration and general office duties involving, typing, and record and file maintenance, documentation creation. 
  • Assisting with sales reporting and documentation 
  • Maintaining relevant EDI information and processing EDI errors 
  • Working closely with customers and internal departments to resolve order and/or inventory issues 
  • Provides general administrative support to the District Manager and Sales positions 
  • Generates and maintains confidential information and administers company policy and procedures 
  • Manage the daily operation of the office, which will include mail and telephone reception 

 

Minimum Qualifications: 

  • High School Diploma or GED 
  • 2 years minimum of administrative support experience 
  • Immigration Sponsorship not offered for this role 
  • Strong PC skills including demonstrated proficiency in Microsoft Excel (VLOOKUP, Pivot tables) 

 

Preferred Qualifications: 

  • Bachelor’s Degree completed or in progress 
  • SAP experience 
  • EDI Customer Portals – Ex. ARIBA, OpenInvoice, iSupplier, etc. 
  • Requires the ability to exercise independent judgment and basic reasoning skills. 
  • Excellent Verbal and Written skills a must. 
  • Ability to work in fast paced environment. 
  • Strong organizational skills and multitasking abilities a must. 
  • Must be flexible, responsive and resourceful 

 

About Us: 

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. 

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. 

Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Job Posting Description

ChampionX has an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in Mount Carmel, IL.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil and gas wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $28-$29/hr
  • Up to $2,000 a week

What You Will Do:

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of Mount Carmel, IL
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of Mount Carmel
  • Relief travel positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • 10-12 hour day time shifts
  • Some regional travel as needed

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A Commercial's Drivers License, issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $95,000 - $110,000 per year!
     

About ChampionX

ChampionX is a global leader in providing chemistry programs and services, drilling technology, artificial lift solutions, and automation technologies for the upstream and midstream oil and gas industry. Our world-class safety culture fuels our purpose to improve lives through our commitment to deliver sustainable operations.

Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion 

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.

job.title: Plant Operator I
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Plant Operator located in Fawley, Southampton. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

·    The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

·    The support of an organisation that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.

·    Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.

·    Comprehensive benefits package & flexible working arrangements that enable balancing work commitments with other priorities.

What You Will Do:

·    Maintain a tidy and safe working environment that meets the Goal Zero and Life Critical Rules set by the company.

·    Take an active role in Autonomous Maintenance ensuring areas are maintained in an appropriate state of cleanliness/order and condition.

·    Be able and active in making recommendations for improvements to practices and procedures in their area of work.

·    To be aware of, understand and display all Quality and Safety procedures to ensure high quality product is produced, shipped and received by the customer on time.

·    To be responsible for the safe operation of blending units on the Fawley site, ensuring quality, housekeeping and environmental standards are maintained at the highest level in accordance to company policy.

·    To be aware of the Permit to Work system on the Fawley site.

·    To be fully conversant with all safety and emergency procedures involving the Fawley Site such that they can become a member of the Site response Team and know what to do in the event of an incident/emergency – Emergency Preparedness.

Minimum Qualifications:

·    Numeracy and literacy skills are required, alongside a good attention to detail.

·    Previous experience in Plant Operations.

·    A team player who is keen to learn and able to adapt to changing scenarios while always maintaining and improving safety standards.

·    Forklift driving is a requirement and the specific training for this is provided.

Physical Demands:

·    This role requires working in a chemical manufacturing plant and is based on the Fawley refinery.

·    As part of your role, you are required to work a shift pattern that includes night shifts. You will also be expected to participate in the weekend duty technician rota as required by the business.

 

Preferred Qualifications/ Experience:

·    Previous exposure to operating chemical blenders and reactors processes.

job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

job.title: Chemical Service Technician
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate and future hiring needs for Chemical Service Technicians in Hobbs,NM.  If you are a strong operation professional with a proven track record of success working in oilfield, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!

What Will You Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  

  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.

  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.

  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.

  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.

  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 10%

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent

  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record

  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle.

  • No immigration sponsorship offered

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s Degree or Trade School Certification

  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience

  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude

  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience

  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.

  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation

  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

job.title: Local HazMat CDL Driver
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We have an immediate need for an Operational Specialist in Plainville, KS.  If you are a dedicated DOT driving professional with a proven track record of success in customer delivery, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base hourly rate and benefits. 

What's in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits
  • Access to best in class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • Comprehensive benefits package (Medical, Dental, Vision, 401K and much more) that starts Day 1
  • $4,000 Hiring Bonus

What You Will Do:

This position will primarily be responsible for providing chemical treatments to oil wells, and the maintenance and safe operation of any type of vehicle used to deliver chemicals to customers, plants and/or warehouse locations.

  • Chemical treating of all scheduled wells and locations on a regular basis
  • Maintain chemical inventory reports
  • Present a professional manner and work closely with salespeople to satisfy customer requirements.
  • Maintain regular communication with sales and supervisor in order to report field problems and/or safety hazards
  • Maintain truck and equipment in a safe and operable condition by performing daily and periodic mechanical and safety inspections
  • Maintain driver's hours of service by using Turnpike and/or paper logbook reporting
  • Keeps current on safety techniques and take advantage of all opportunities to improve driving and safety skills
  • Recognize and avoid safety hazards in the work environment
  • Properly load trucks, prepare daily bills of lading, schedule the order of daily treatments and submit treating reports to sales

Territory / Location Information:

  • This will be a local position that will be based in and will be responsible for chemical treatment and delivery within a 50-mile radius of McCook, NE
  • Candidate must reside within 50 miles of McCook, NE
  • Relief positions also available with a $2.00 an hour travel bonus

Schedule:

  • Multiple Schedules available
  • Monday Thru Friday with an occasional Saturday
  • Weekend rotation availability with days off in the week
  • 10-12 hour day time shifts

Basic/Minimum Qualifications:

  • Minimum high school graduate or GED
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate company vehicle
  • Must currently hold a valid Class A or B Commercial Driver's License (CDL), issued by the state in which the candidate resides
  • Must have HazMat and Tanker Endorsements or be willing to be obtain within 30 days
  • One (1) year of commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past three years required
  • Acceptable motor vehicle record (3 years)
  • All candidates must have the ability to speak, read and write English
  • Immigration sponsorship is not offered for this role
  • Must be comfortable working with cameras in the truck cab
  • No manual transmission restrictions

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Carry up to 50 pounds chest high.
  • Must be able to pass a drug screen and physical exam

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Three (3) years commercial driving experience, compliant with Federal Motor Carrier Safety Regulations and DOT regulations, within the past five years required
  • Experience with the operation of forklifts, drum dollies, trailer hoists, chemical pumps, hoses, meters, printers and gauges
  • Experience with handling products in various types of packages including bulk, drums, Porta-Feeds and mini/micro-feed units
  • Knowledge of precautions needed if exposed to H2S (hydrogen sulfide)
  • Experience in customer relations
  • Experience performing warehouse duties, which include preventative maintenance, inventory and ordering of chemicals, receiving incoming shipments, organizing warehouse for safe and efficient chemical storage, operation of customized chemical manufacturing equipment and the upkeep of warehouse grounds
  • Awareness of chemical safety and the measures to take in the event of skin contact or spill

Position Compensation and Benefits

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly and overtime pay earning potential between $75,000 to $95,000 a year!

job.title: Supervisor - Drive Shop VSD Repair
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for a Variable Speed Drive Supervisor located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s In It For You:

  • Flexible, independent work environment where you will manage a monthly schedule
  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Grow your income as you drive growth
  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment
  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Supervise daily activities of VSD technicians, including assembly, programming, testing, troubleshooting, and maintenance
  • Ensure safe and efficient workflow aligned with manufacturing and ESP operational goals
  • Coordinate with Engineering, Applications, Quality, and Supply Chain to resolve product issues and implement process improvements
  • Review and interpret electrical schematics, wiring diagrams, single-line diagrams, and PLC/VFD documentation to support technical troubleshooting
  • Lead root-cause analysis for equipment failures, electrical issues, and production defects
  • Maintain ERP-driven workflows for production orders, inventory accuracy, BOM validation, and work order completion
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives aimed at reducing downtime, improving quality, and enhancing operational throughput
  • Train and develop VSD personnel in product knowledge, safe work practices, and troubleshooting methods
  • Ensure compliance with company safety policies and electrical standards

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 5 years of experience working with VSDs/VFDs in manufacturing, electrical assembly, artificial lift, or industrial equipment settings
  • Experience in the ESP industry (service, engineering, repair, or manufacturing).
  • Proficiency with ERP systems – including Oracle, SAP, or similar – for work order management, parts consumption, and production tracking
  • Ability to read and interpret wiring diagrams, ladder logic, I/O schematics, and electrical drawings
  • Strong troubleshooting capabilities for VSDs, electrical panels, control circuits, and instrumentation
  • Proven ability to collaborate cross-functionally with Applications Engineering, Product Engineering, Quality, and Operations
  • Strong leadership skills with experience supervising teams or leading technical workflows
  • Excellent communication, organizational, and documentation skills
  • Proficiency with Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, Word PowerPoint)
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to stand, bend, lift, and move throughout the manufacturing floor
  • Comfortable working around energized electrical equipment under proper safe-work practices
  • May require overtime or off-shift support based on production needs

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s or Bachelor’s degree in Electrical Engineering, Industrial Technology, or related field
  • Experience with ESP surface equipment, switchboards, and artificial lift control systems
  • Familiarity with PLC programming concepts or HMI interfaces Background in continuous improvement, Lean, or root-cause analysis methodologies

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. job.title: Sr. Chemist
job.description:
Job Description

Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

This Senior Chemist position focuses on applying advanced chemical expertise to design and execute laboratory testing, analyze complex data, and support research and development activities. The role requires strong leadership, including opportunities to mentor junior chemists, guiding technical workstreams, and driving best practices in safety, quality, and efficiency, while managing a high-volume workload of up to 1,000 samples per month. Working closely with a small, collaborative team of two other chemists and reporting directly to the Regional Lab Manager, this individual must prioritize teamwork and effective communication to ensure project success. The ideal candidate brings 5+ years of laboratory experience, proven leadership ability, and strong problem-solving skills within a highly team-oriented environment.

The successful candidate will be a technically strong analytical chemist who thrives in a small-team environment, is comfortable managing a high-volume analytical workload, takes ownership of responsibilities, communicates effectively with customers and colleagues, and contributes positively to a collaborative and accountable laboratory culture.

Minimum Education & Experience Requirements

  • Bachelor's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 3 years of production chemistry experience, or

  • Master's degree in Chemistry or related field with at least 1 years of production chemistry experience

  • Demonstrated experience supporting production chemistry programs for U.S. land-based oil and gas operations.

  • Experience managing high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, and turnaround time expectations.

  • Proven ability to follow SOPs, work independently, prioritize competing demands, and manage moderately complex projects with minimal supervision.

  • Experience operating and troubleshooting ICP-OES, Ion Chromatography (IC), Gas Chromatography (GC), XRF, or equivalent analytical instrumentation.

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint, etc) and Laboratory Information Management Systems (LIMS), with experience leveraging data management and reporting tools to support laboratory operations, decision-making, and process improvement.

Required Skills & Experience (Must – Haves)

  • Demonstrated success working within a small laboratory team where collaboration, flexibility, and individual accountability are critical to overall laboratory performance.

  • Demonstrate initiative and ability to work independently and handle multiple tasks.

  • Demonstrated ability to adapt to changing priorities while maintaining a strong commitment to safety, quality, and customer responsiveness.

  • Demonstrated desire and initiative to grow into leadership roles over time, including taking on increased responsibility, mentoring peers, and contributing to team development.

  • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills with exceptional attention to detail.

  • Effective verbal and written communication skills with the ability to communicate technical information to both technical and non-technical audiences.

  • Physically able to lift 50 pounds, climb stairs or ladder, and agile enough to squat and bend over.

  • Ability to independently prioritize tasks, manage multiple projects, and meet deadlines with minimal supervision.

  • Strong organizational skills and ability to manage a high-volume workload (handling up to 1,000 samples per month).

Preferred Skills & Experience (Nice to Have)

  • 3-5 years of experience in oilfield production chemistry, chemical manufacturing, or industrial analytical laboratory environments.

  •  3+ years of laboratory leadership, technical leadership, mentoring, or senior laboratory experience supporting production chemistry operations.

  •  Demonstrated history of taking ownership of laboratory responsibilities, following through on commitments, and consistently delivering high-quality results.

  • Proven ability to foster a collaborative, respectful, and solutions-oriented work environment through effective communication and constructive engagement with colleagues and stakeholders.

  • Experience presenting technical findings, managing projects, and collaborating effectively across functional teams.

  • Understanding of customer needs and ability to translate analytical data into actionable operational insights.

  • Experience identifying and implementing process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, or customer service. 

Job Duties:

  • Lead and execute routine and non-routine analytical testing activities in support of customer programs, field operations, and laboratory service commitments.

  • Apply advanced production chemistry knowledge and sound technical judgment to troubleshoot challenges, validate methods, interpret analytical data, and solve complex problems.

  • Support customer programs by designing, conducting, and interpreting laboratory analyses tied to real-world field performance and operational outcomes.

  • Independently manage multiple priorities, project deadlines, customer requests, and stakeholder communications in a fast-paced laboratory environment.

  • Manage high volumes of routine analytical testing while maintaining quality, accuracy, data integrity, and turnaround time expectations.

  • Operate, maintain, troubleshoot, and optimize laboratory instrumentation while ensuring compliance with established QA/QC procedures and laboratory standards.

  • Support the mentoring, training, and development of chemists, technicians, and technologists through coaching, knowledge sharing, and technical guidance.

  • Collaborate with field personnel, sales, technical teams, and customers to translate analytical data into actionable recommendations.

  • Identify opportunities for process improvements that enhance laboratory efficiency, quality, safety, and customer service.

  • Champion safety culture, support continuous improvement initiatives, and contribute positively to overall laboratory performance, efficiency, and team effectiveness.

Perform miscellaneous administrative tasks.

About ChampionX

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a

balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators,

that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future

with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going

job.title: Document Design and Control
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has an immediate need for a Document Designer/Controller in Freeport, Texas to support our manufacturing operations. This is an exciting opportunity to join a growing organization that values safety, operational excellence, continuous improvement, and collaboration. As a Document Designer/Controller, you will play a key role in maintaining accurate engineering documentation, supporting capital and maintenance projects, and ensuring plant design records are properly managed and maintained.

What's in it For You:

· Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous education

· Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety, including top-notch safety training and personal protective equipment

· Pride in working for a company that provides clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments

· Opportunity to work with a collaborative team supporting engineering, operations, maintenance, and capital projects

· Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching, paid time off, and more

What You Will Do:

· Conduct field walkdowns in various weather conditions to gather information to create and/or confirm as-built piping drawings in the plant/production facility.

· Generate PFDs, P&IDs, 3D modeling, plot plans, details, and ISOs.

· Demonstrate a working understanding of a chemical manufacturing site.

· Effectively communicate and work with engineers and construction crews to successfully complete projects.

· Successfully complete small, fast-paced projects on time and within budget.

· Work in a team environment, taking direction from department leads and efficiently prioritizing work to produce timely and accurate project deliverables.

· Work with Engineers and Contractors to assign and physically identify equipment numbers for new and existing assets.

· Maintain knowledge and organization of Piping Line and Specialty Item Lists.

· Maintain the Line Number and Instrument/Equipment Tag Number Master Database.

· Revise drawings and engineering markups as necessary.

· Manage the filing and maintenance of hard copy engineering drawings.

· Maintain vessel, tank, exchanger, relief device, and piping inspection spreadsheets.

· Track inspection types (internal/external), vendors, inspectors, and certifications.

· Verify records are filed both electronically and physically.

· Plan and track project and turnaround activities using Microsoft planning tools (e.g., Microsoft Project, Microsoft Planner), while supporting team training and onboarding to ensure effective use of scheduling systems and workflows.

· Follow all company safety procedures and protocols.

· Perform all other duties as assigned.

Position Details:

· Primarily plant-based position supporting engineering, operations, and maintenance teams.

· Regular field walkdowns in an operating chemical manufacturing facility.

· Must be able to work in varying weather conditions while conducting field verification activities.

· Collaborate with multiple departments, including Technical, Fixed Equipment, Operations, Capital Projects, and Contractors.

Minimum Qualifications:

· Associate's degree in CAD or a related field preferred.

· Minimum of five years of piping design experience in the chemical industry.

· Proficiency with applicable industry codes and standards.

· Proficiency in AutoCAD Plant 3D and/or Cadworx 3D.

· Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, including Outlook, Word, and Excel.

Knowledge and Skills:

· Excellent communication skills.

· Experience working with various departments, including Technical, Fixed Equipment, Operations, Capital Projects, and Contractors.

· General knowledge of industry piping design standards and the ability to learn and apply plant standards as required.

· Experience using Microsoft Project, Microsoft Planner, or similar planning tools.

· Strong attention to detail.

· Self-motivated and independently driven.

· Strong organizational and document control skills.

· Ability to manage multiple priorities while maintaining accurate engineering documentation.

Preferred Qualifications:

· Experience supporting document control within a chemical manufacturing or industrial plant environment.

· Experience maintaining engineering drawing databases and inspection records.

· Experience supporting turnaround planning and capital projects.

· Familiarity with equipment tagging systems, piping line lists, and engineering document management processes.

Physical Demands:

· Ability to perform field walkdowns throughout an operating manufacturing facility.

· Ability to work in varying outdoor weather conditions.

· Ability to climb stairs, walk extended distances, and wear required personal protective equipment (PPE).

· Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that's been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative changemakers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Material Handler I
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

ChampionX has immediate and future hiring needs for a Warehouse / Material Operator to support manufacturing and warehouse operations. This is an opportunity to join a growing organization focused on safety, operational excellence, and continuous improvement, offering a competitive compensation package and comprehensive benefits.

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, technology, and continuous learning opportunities
  • Thrive in a company that values a strong safety culture, including top-tier safety training and personal protective equipment
  • Pride in working for a company that supports clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments
  • Opportunity to build skills across warehouse, inventory, and production operations
  • Comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, and 401(k) with company matching and more

What You Will Do:

  • Exhibit safe work habits and comply with all company safety regulations and guidelines
  • Assist with material movement throughout warehouse and manufacturing areas
  • Perform shipping and receiving activities for order fulfillment and inventory replenishment
  • Load and unload containerized and bulk materials from transport trucks, including sampling and required approvals
  • Stage drums, totes, and materials for production and shipment
  • Operate forklifts and RF scanning devices to move, track, and store materials
  • Conduct daily forklift inspections and ensure safe operation of equipment
  • Maintain accurate inventory control, labeling of goods, and sample management
  • Support quality improvement processes and participate in team-based initiatives
  • Apply spill containment and cleanup procedures when required
  • Ensure vendors and contractors adhere to plant safety policies
  • Maintain a clean, organized, and safe working environment
  • Understand key performance indicators (KPIs) and contribute to team performance goals
  • Participate in safety meetings, planning committees, and team functions
  • Learn and apply Continuous Improvement (CI) principles in daily tasks
  • Perform additional duties as assigned

Position Details:

  • Work Environment: On-site, supporting warehouse and manufacturing operations
  • Ability to work various shifts as required
  • Role is safety-sensitive

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High School Diploma or GED
  • 6 months of relevant experience in warehouse, manufacturing, or logistics environment
  • Ability to operate a forklift or willingness to become certified
  • Ability to operate handheld or mounted computer devices (RF scanners, etc.)
  • Must be able to speak, read, and write English
  • Willingness to wear required PPE, including respirators and fall protection
  • Immigration sponsorship not offered for this role

Physical Demands:

  • Ability to lift and carry up to 50 pounds
  • Ability to bend, squat, reach overhead, and perform physical tasks
  • Ability to climb stairs and ladders
  • Role may be subject to employer or customer drug testing

Preferred Qualifications:

  • 1 year of experience in a manufacturing or warehouse environment
  • Forklift certification
  • Experience with SAP or similar inventory management systems
  • Familiarity with Continuous Improvement or Lean principles
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to work effectively in a team environment

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We unlock access to energy for the benefit of all while advancing a future with more energy and less carbon. Our diverse and forward-thinking teams are committed to developing the technologies needed to power the future responsibly.

job.title: Application Engineer II
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Applications Engineer located in Midland, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and benefits

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology

  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path

  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection

What You Will Do:

  • Develop technical proposals of how the organization's products and services can meet customer needs and be integrated and implemented with the customer's existing systems and equipment.

  • Develop and deliver sales presentations and respond to technical questions regarding the organization's product, system, or service.

  • Estimate the costs, resources, time, and technical requirements of modifying, configuring, and installing the organization's products and services to meet customer needs.

  • Liaise with hardware, software, and systems design engineers to ensure that products and services are modified, configured, and installed according to customer needs.

  • Respond to customer questions and troubleshoot, investigate, and resolve technical problems that arise during or after implementation.

  • Train customers on the features of the product or service they have purchased.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering or equivalent experience

  • 2 years of experience

  • Candidate must be authorized to work in the US, no Visa sponsorship offered for this position.

  • Expertise to provide technical advice during larger or difficult negotiations, or with customer who are of strategic importance to the organization.

  • Interact politely and professionally with customers and /or coworkers

  • Strong customer skills including the ability to explain and train.

  • Follow all safety policies and company-wide safety requirements; encourage action assuring safe behavior; confront unsafe behavior and conditions proactively and positively

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Work in an unsupervised environment

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

  • Use a computer to communicate, create, and access data

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. 

  • Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Exerting up to 10 pounds of force occasionally and/or a negligible amount of force frequently or constantly to lift, carry, push, pull, or otherwise move objects, including the human body.

  • Sedentary work involves sitting most of the time.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Sitting: May sit for extended periods of time.

  • Moving around the office: Moving between desk and file cabinets and/or office equipment.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: preparing and analyzing data and figures; transcribing; viewing a computer terminal;

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

job.description:

ChampionX (SLB) has an immediate need for a Pump Repair Technician located in Roosevelt, UT.

This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers through continuous improvement.

Shop Operator

job.title: RD&E Group Leader
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are searching for an RD&E Group Leader for our Diagnostic Solutions team located in Sugar Land, Texas.  As a Group Leader, you will align resources and prioritize projects amongst your team to ensure targets are met. You will act as a liaison with internal and external customers to define scopes of work and communicate project updates. The ideal candidate is highly versatile, communicates effectively, thrives in a collaborative environment, and has a strong track record of leading project teams to deliver technical solutions to complex problems. 

What's in it For You:

  • A flexible, independent work environment where you will manage a monthly schedule

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools and technology

  • Thrive in a company that values a culture of safety to include top-notch safety training and personal protection equipment

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, a company-paid pension plan, tuition reimbursement and more!

What You Will Do:

In this role, as RD&E Group Leader for the Diagnostic Solutions team, your primary responsibility is assisting and enabling your teams to provide materials compatibility and analytical support for our customers and researchers. This is a leadership position, and the successful candidate will be responsible for the safety, quality, productivity and talent development of their teams.

Main responsibilities:

  • Provide leadership and technical expertise to the Materials Compatibility team and the Waters, Deposits, and Hydrocarbons Analytical team

  • Lead the team's technical effort under the guidance of the senior leadership, utilizing experience in fundamental science, oil field problem solving, chemical manufacturing/scale-up support, and project/people management

  • Ensure each associate performs duties in a safe and efficient manner, maintains quality standards, and continues to grow and develop expertise

  • Provide materials compatibility and analytical support for internal stakeholders and the company’s customers

  • Apply performance management guidelines for setting performance goals, evaluating and promoting career growth and development, conducting performance appraisals, and recommending salary and performance adjustments.

  • Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, and Technology teams to scope projects and identify needs/gaps to feed future projects

  • Work closely with team members to help ensure effective and efficient operations

  • Engage with key stakeholders to forecast sample and project demand to enable effective resource allocation to meet business timelines

  • Identify opportunities for third-party testing to reduce sample backlogs and expand testing capabilities as needed

  • Manage several projects at once while working within a fast-paced environment

  • Hire and train new team members as needed

Position Details: This position is located in Sugar Land, Texas.

Minimum Qualifications:

  • Degree in analytical chemistry, materials science, polymer science or related scientific/technical discipline (required, and relevant experience including technology strategy development & implementation, leading & developing teams, and driving innovation. Education and experience should fall in one of the following levels:

    • Bachelors degree and 7 years experience; OR

    • Masters degree and 4 years experience; OR

    • PhD and 2 years experience

  • Five or more years of relevant experiences in management, technology development and innovation areas.

  • Requires track record of successful technology development and leveraging of external technology.

  • Ability to motivate individuals to operate as a part of a high-functioning team

  • Ability to travel up to 20%

Preferred Qualifications:

  • For the Materials Compatibility team, experience with at least one of the following techniques is highly desired: Tensile, Hardness, measurement of corrosion rates, high pressure (2000 psi), high temperature (500 °F) immersion testing, failure analysis, electrochemistry (critical pitting temperature and anodic cyclic polarization).

  • For the Waters, Deposits, and Hydrocarbons Analytical team, experience with common analytical techniques such as ICP, IC, XRF, XRD, SEM, and GC is highly desired.

  • Excellent time management skills

  • Excellent written, oral, and visual communication skills to communicate effectively with all levels of the organization, both within and outside the business

  • Previous supervisory experience is preferred

  • Experience with Specialty Chemicals in the Oil & Gas industry is preferred

About Us

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there. For more news and information, visit SLB.com. job.title: Chemical Service Technician
job.description:
isclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

Job Posting Description

ChampionX has immediate and future needs for a Chemical Service Technician located in Williston, ND. If you are a strong operation professional with a proven track record of success working in oilfield, we invite you to apply.  This is your opportunity to join a large growing company offering a competitive base salary and benefits. 

What’s in it For You:

  • Access to best-in-class resources, tools, and technology
  • Opportunity for a long term, advanced career path
  • A culture that values safety first, including training and personal protection
  • You will join a growing company offering competitive pay and comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, matching 401(k) with company matching and more!
  • Hourly Rate of $28-$32

What Will You Do:

  • Provide routine service support to Sales Representatives in strategic district accounts to execute agreed upon customer system assurance plans.  
  • Assist Sales Representatives with start-ups of new applications in both existing customers and in new accounts.
  • Provide technical support to customers; identifying and resolving customer pain points, escalating as required.
  • Execute required chemistry tests, log, and report data, and maintain product inventories at assigned customer accounts.
  • Install, calibrate, and maintain chemical feed equipment and process monitoring equipment at assigned customer accounts.
  • Possible travel within assigned sales territory 10%

Minimum Qualifications:

  • High school diploma or equivalent
  • Must have a valid Driver's License and acceptable Motor Vehicle Record
  • Must be 21 years of age or older to operate a company vehicle.
  • No immigration sponsorship offered

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Associate’s Degree or Trade School Certification
  • 2 years of successful technical service or field sales support experience
  • Must have a strong mechanical aptitude
  • Water treatment or specialty chemical industry experience
  • Working knowledge of boilers, cooling towers, and wastewater treatment systems.
  • Practical computer application literacy (including Microsoft Office Suite and ability to learn internal business systems)
  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation
  • Prior experience that demonstrates a strong work ethic and ability to multi-task

Physical Demands:

  • Must have the ability to Lift/Push/Pull/Carry up to 55 pounds chest high.
  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

job.title: Business Development Manager - Mexico and Colombia
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an experience Business Development Manager for RMSpumptools located in Houston, TX. The Business Development Manager will be responsible for driving Artificial Lift sales and RMSpumptools projects in Mexico and Colombia, as well as being a Global Ambassador for RMSpumptools. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth.
  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best.
  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement.
  • Comprehensive benefits package & flexible working arrangements that enable balancing work commitments with other priorities.

What You Will Do:

  • Researching organizations and developing plans for business and revenue growth in the Artificial Lift Market in Mexico and Colombia

  • Finding and developing new markets, improving sales and potential project work

  • Developing technical quotes, and proposals for clients. 

  • Representing RMSpumptools – their technologies, expertise and values / reputation

  • Researching, planning, and implementing new target market initiatives 

  • Identify and develop strategies for further market penetration.

  • Pursuing leads and moving them through the sales cycle 

  • Articulate value story, technology, and product positioning to both business and technical users

  • Attend and participate in tear down and equipment inspection and reports across the Mexico & Colombia Regions

  • Effectively develop and execute regional sales plans to support the strategic growth objectives while maintaining a profitable gross-margin for the region 

  • Deliver monthly, quarterly and annual sales in the region with high levels of accountability, integrity, and ethics 

  • Communicate with the Sales Team and the Production Team regularly to guarantee that sales and client objectives are met 

  • Provide product training at the field and corporate levels for all customer types 

  • Provide periodic sales activity report as required 

  • Manage a geo-region to deliver sales revenues, customer support and objectives as defined by the Director of Sales 

  • Develop and update reliable sales forecasts 

  • Identify, qualify, and convert project and new customer sales opportunities that will contribute to the territory revenues 

  • Promote integrity and ethical behavior with customers, partners, and employees according to RMS policies 

Position Details: 

  • Work week: Monday through Friday, weekends as needed for customer account management 

  • Region includes Mexico, Colombia and some areas within the Andes region.

  • Willingness to travel up to 40% in the specified sales territory

  • Travel up to 40% (both air and land) 

  • Regular attendance and punctuality are expected upon accepting this position for customer meetings, business meetings and trainings, etc. 

  • Properly follow company and safety procedures

  • Experience and knowledge of the Mexican and Colombian Artificial Lift Market

Minimum Qualifications:

  • 10 years of proven sales experience in Artificial Lift, Sub Sea or a related area 

  • Bachelors degree in Engineering or relevant industry experience

  • Fluent in Spanish and English

  • Strong oral and written communication skills, including the ability to present, influence, and collaborate at the leadership level. 

  • Strong technical expertise in oil and gas automated lift 

  • Results Oriented - Good follow up skills to ensure strategy implementation 

  • History of working in a field that required autonomy and self-motivation.

  • Ability to present, influence, and collaborate at the leadership level.

  • Excellent organizational skills to meet goals and set priorities

  • Be proactive, organized and handle work under uncertain environments.

  • Defining, implementing (and innovating) sales plans

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

job.title: Inventory Coordinator
job.description:
Disclaimer: SLB completed the acquisition of ChampionX on July 16, 2025. If selected for this position, your employment will begin with ChampionX and will transition to SLB as part of the planned integration.

We are looking for an Inventory Coordinator located in Odessa, TX. This is your opportunity to join a global technology company, driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

The Inventory Coordinator works closely with Operations, Logistics, and Warehouse teams to ensure product availability, optimize inventory accuracy, and support customer and business requirements.

What’s in it For You:

  • The ability to make an impact and shape your career with a company that is passionate about growth

  • The support of an organization that believes it is vital to include and engage diverse people, perspectives, and ideas to achieve our best

  • Thrive in a company that values sustainability, drives a safety-focused culture and empowers its employees through continuous improvement

  • Comprehensive benefits package that includes medical, dental, vision, 401(k) with company matching and more!

What You Will Do:

  • Performed daily inventory cycle counts, investigated and resolved inventory discrepancies, and reported findings to management.

  • Received incoming parts, verified quantities and descriptions against vendor packing lists, and ensured accuracy before processing.

  • Used Oracle to receive inventory invoices, confirming quantities matched both the packing list and system records.

  • Used Apex to process parts requests from different branches, pull parts from inventory, packed and sealed shipments, and prepared orders using UPS or RXO.

  • Monitored inventory levels and restocked shelves to maintain adequate stock.

  • Completed weekly vendor runs to pick up parts.

  • Operated forklifts to safely load and unload freight trucks and move materials throughout the yard.

  • Maintained a clean, organized, and hazard-free work environment in compliance with safety standards.

Minimum Qualifications:  

  • High School education or equivalent

  • 2 years relevant experience managing inventory

  • Experience using Oracle or SAP

  • Ability to drive a forklift

  • Attention to detail

  • Good verbal and written communication skills

  • Basic computer skills, including Microsoft Office Suite

  • Work in excess of 8 hours per day and 40 hours per week, as required to meet business needs

  • Read, write, and speak English fluently

Physical Demands:

  • The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job.  Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.

  • Role is deemed safety-sensitive and may be subject to employer or customer drug testing.

  • Medium work:  Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects.

  • Climbing:  Ascending or descending ladders, stairs, scaffolding, ramps, poles and the like, using feet and legs and/or hands and arms. Body agility is emphasized.

  • Balancing:  Maintaining body equilibrium to prevent falling when walking, standing, or crouching on narrow, slippery, or erratically moving surfaces.

  • Stooping:  Bending body downward and forward by bending spine at the waist. This factor is important if it occurs to a considerable degree and requires full use of the lower extremities and back muscles.

  • Kneeling:  Bending legs at knee to come to a rest on knees.

  • Crouching:  Bending the body downward and forward by bending leg and spine.

  • Crawling:  Moving about on hands and knees or hands and feet.

  • Reaching:  Extending hand(s) and arm(s) in any direction.

  • Walking:  Moving about on foot to accomplish tasks, particularly for long distances or moving from one work site to another.

  • Pushing:  Using upper extremities to press against something with steady force in order to thrust forward, downward, or outward.

  • Pulling:  Using upper extremities to exert force in order to draw, drag, haul, or tug objects in a sustained motion.

  • Lifting:  Raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally from position to position.

  • Fingering:  Picking, pinching, typing, or otherwise working, primarily with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm as in handling.

  • Grasping:  Applying pressure to an object with the fingers and palm.

  • Feeling:  Perceiving attributes of objects, such as size, shape, temperature, or texture by touching with skin, particularly that of fingertips.

  • Talking:  Expressing or exchanging ideas by means of the spoken word.  Those activities in which they must convey detailed or important spoken instructions to customer or other workers accurately, loudly, or quickly.

  • Hearing:  Perceiving the nature of sounds at normal speaking levels with or without correction.  Ability to receive detailed information through oral communication, and to make the discriminations in sound.

  • Repetitive motion:  Substantial movement (motions) of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers to use a computer.

  • The employee is required to have close visual acuity to perform an activity such as: visual inspection involving small defects, small parts and/or operation of machines (including inspection), using measurement devices, and/or assembly or fabrication of parts at distances close to the eyes.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to operate motor vehicles.

  • The employee is required to have visual acuity to determine the accuracy, neatness, thoroughness of work assigned or to make general observations of facilities or structures.

About Us:

ChampionX is now part of SLB, a global technology company driving energy innovation for a balanced planet.

We are a technology company that unlocks access to energy for the benefit of all. As innovators, that’s been our mission for nearly a century. Today, we face a global imperative to create a future with more energy, but less carbon. Our diverse, innovative change makers are focused on going further in innovation and inventing the new energy technologies we need to get there.

For more news and information, visit SLB.com

Follow us on Facebook at @WeAreSLBGlobal, LinkedIn: SLBGlobal, or Instagram at @SLBGlobal

We believe the best teams are diverse and inclusive, and we are on a journey to create a workplace where every employee can grow and achieve their best. We are committed to fair and equal treatment of employees and applicants. We recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement on the basis of individual qualifications and job performance. In all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement, we will not discriminate against any employee or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin, citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, disability, or status as a covered veteran.
